text.NRSV: All wisdom is from the Lord, and with him it remains forever.
text.DRB: All wisdom is from the Lord God, and hath been always with him, and is before all time.
text.KJV: 1:1 This Iesus was the sonne of Sirach, and grand-childe to Iesus of the same name with him; This man therefore liued in the latter times, after the people had bene led away captiue, and called home againe, and almost after all the Prophets. Now his grandfather Iesus (as he himselfe witnesseth) was a man of great diligence and wisedome among the Hebrewes, who did not onely gather the graue and short Sentences of wise men, that had bene before him, but himselfe also vttered some of his owne, full of much vnderstanding and wisedome. When as therefore the first Iesus died, leauing this booke almost perfected, Si rach his sonne receiuing it after him, left it to his owne sonne Iesus, who hauing gotten it into his hands, compiled it all orderly into one Volume, and called it Wisdome, Intituling it, both by his owne name, his fathers name, and his grandfathers, alluring the hearer by the very name of Wisedome, to haue a greater loue to the studie of this Booke. It conteineth therefore wise Sayings, darke Sentences, and Parables, and certaine particular ancient godly stories of men that pleased God. Also his Prayer and Song. Moreouer, what benefits God had vouchsafed his people, and what plagues he had heaped vpon their enemies. This Iesus did imitate Solomon, and was no lesse famous for Wisedome, and learning, both being indeed a man of great learning, and so reputed also. Whereas many and great things haue bene deliuered vnto vs by the Law and the Prophets, and by others that haue followed their steps, for the which things Israel ought to be commended for learning and Wisedome, and whereof not onely the Readers must needs become skilful themselues, but also they that desire to learne, be able to profit them which are without, both by speaking and writing : My grandfather Iesus, when he had much giuen himselfe to the reading of the Law, and the Prophets, and other Bookes of our fathers, and had gotten therein good iudgement, was drawen on also himselfe, to write something pertayning to learning and Wisedome, to the intent that those which are desirous to learne, and are addicted to these things, might profit much more in liuing according to the Law. Wherefore, let me intreat you to reade it with fauour and attention, and to pardon Vs, wherein wee may secme to come short of some words which we haue laboured to interprete. For the same things vttered in Hebrew, and translated into an other tongue, haue not the same force in them : and not onely these things, but the Law it selfe, and the Prophets, and the rest of the Bookes, haue no small difference, when they are spoken in their owne language. For in the eight and thirtieth yeere comming into Egypt, when Euergetes was King, and continuing there some time, I found a Booke of no small learning, therefore I thought it most necessary for mee, to bestow some diligence and trauaile to interprete it: Vsing great watchfulnesse, and skill in that space, to bring the Booke to an end, and set it foorth for them also, which in a strange countrey are willing to learne, being prepared before in maners to line after the Law. All wisedome commeth from the Lord, and is with him for euer.
text.NABRE: 1:1 Foreword - Inasmuch as many and great truths have been given to us through the Law, the prophets, and the authors who followed them, for which the instruction and wisdom of Israel merit praise, it is the duty of those who read the scriptures not only to become knowledgeable themselves but also to use their love of learning in speech and in writing to help others less familiar. So my grandfather Jesus, who had long devoted himself to the study of the law, the prophets, and the rest of the books of our ancestors, and had acquired great familiarity with them, was moved to write something himself regarding instruction and wisdom. He did this so that those who love learning might, by accepting what he had written, make even greater progress in living according to the Law. You are invited therefore to read it with good will and attention, with indulgence for any failure on our part, despite earnest efforts, in the interpretation of particular passages. For words spoken originally in Hebrew do not have the same effect when they are translated into another language. That is true not only of this book but of the Law itself, the prophecies, and the rest of the books, which differ no little when they are read in the original. I arrived in Egypt in the thirty-eighth year of the reign of King Euergetes, and while there, I had access to no little learning. I therefore considered it my duty to devote some diligence and industry to the translation of this book. During this time I applied my skill for many sleepless hours to complete the book and publish it for those living abroad who wish to acquire learning and are disposed to live their lives according to the Law. The Wisdom of Ben Sira Chapter 1 God’s Gift of Wisdom - All wisdom is from the Lord and remains with him forever.

text.NRSV: The sand of the sea, the drops of rain, and the days of eternity—who can count them?
text.DRB: Who hath numbered the sand of the sea, and the drops of rain, and the days of the world? Who hath measured the height of heaven, and the breadth of the earth, and the depth of the abyss?
text.KJV: 1:2 Who can number the sand of the sea, and the drops of raine, and the dayes of eternity?
text.NABRE: 1:2 The sands of the sea, the drops of rain, the days of eternity—who can count them?

text.NRSV: The height of heaven, the breadth of the earth, the abyss, and wisdom—who can search them out?
text.DRB: Who hath searched out the wisdom of God that goeth before all things?
text.KJV: 1:3 Who can finde out the height of heauen, and the breadth of the earth, and the deepe, and wisedome?
text.NABRE: 1:3 Heaven’s height, earth’s extent, the abyss and wisdom—who can explore them?

text.NRSV: Wisdom was created before all other things, and prudent understanding from eternity.
text.DRB: Wisdom hath been created before all things, and the understanding of prudence from everlasting.
text.KJV: 1:4 Wisedome hath beene created before all things, and the vnderstanding of prudence from euerlasting.
text.NABRE: 1:4 Before all other things wisdom was created; and prudent understanding, from eternity.

text.DRB: The word of God on high is the fountain of wisdom, and her ways are everlasting commandments.
text.KJV: 1:5 The word of God most high, is the fountaine of wisdome, & her wayes are euerlasting commandements.

text.NRSV: The root of wisdom—to whom has it been revealed? Her subtleties—who knows them?
text.DRB: To whom hath the root of wisdom been revealed, and who hath known her wise counsels?
text.KJV: 1:6 To whom hath the root of wisdome beene reuealed? or who hath knowen her wise counsels?

text.DRB: To whom hath the discipline of wisdom been revealed and made manifest? and who hath understood the multiplicity of her steps?

text.NRSV: There is but one who is wise, greatly to be feared, seated upon his throne—the Lord.
text.DRB: There is one most high Creator Almighty, and a powerful king, and greatly to be feared, who sitteth upon his throne, and is the God of dominion.
text.KJV: 1:8 There is one wise and greatly to bee feared; the Lord sitting vpon his Throne.

text.NRSV: It is he who created her; he saw her and took her measure; he poured her out upon all his works,
text.DRB: He created her in the Holy Ghost, and saw her, and numbered her, and measured her.
text.KJV: 1:9 He created her, and saw her, and numbred her, and powred her out vpon all his workes.

text.NRSV: upon all the living according to his gift; he lavished her upon those who love him.
text.DRB: And he poured her out upon all his works, and upon all flesh according to his gift, and hath given her to them that love him.
text.KJV: 1:10 Shee with all flesh according to his gift, and hee hath giuen her to them that loue him.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is glory and exultation, and gladness and a crown of rejoicing.
text.DRB: The fear of the Lord is honour, and glory, and gladness, and a crown of joy.
text.KJV: 1:11 The feare of the Lord is honour, and glory, and gladnesse, and a crowne of reioycing.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord delights the heart, and gives gladness and joy and long life.
text.DRB: The fear of the Lord shall delight the heart, and shall give joy, and gladness, and length of days.
text.KJV: 1:12 The feare of the Lord maketh a merrie heart, and giueth ioy and gladnesse, and a long life.

text.DRB: With him that feareth the Lord, it shall go well in the latter end, and in the day of his death he shall be blessed.
text.KJV: 1:13 Who so feareth the Lord, it shall goe well with him at the last, & he shall finde fauour in the day of his death.

text.NRSV: To fear the Lord is the beginning of wisdom; she is created with the faithful in the womb.
text.DRB: The love of God is honourable wisdom.
text.KJV: 1:14 To feare the Lord, is the beginning of wisedome: and it was created with the faithfull in the wombe.

text.DRB: And they to whom she shall shew herself love her by the sight, and by the knowledge of her great works.
text.KJV: 1:15 Shee hath built an euerlasting foundation with men, and she shal continue with their seede.

text.DRB: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom, and was created with the faithful in the womb, it walketh with chosen women, and is known with the just and faithful.
text.KJV: 1:16 To feare the Lord, is fulnesse of wisedome, and filleth men with her fruits.

text.DRB: The fear of the Lord is the religiousness of knowledge.
text.KJV: 1:17 Shee filleth all their house with things desireable, and the garners with her increase.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is the crown of wisdom, making peace and perfect health to flourish.
text.DRB: Religiousness shall keep and justify the heart, it shall give joy and gladness.
text.KJV: 1:18 The feare of the Lord is a crowne of wisedome, making peace and perfect health to flourish, both which are the gifts of God: and it enlargeth their reioycing that loue him.
1:20 The root of wisedome is to feare the Lord, and the branches thereof are long life.
text.NRSV: To fear the Lord is the root of wisdom, and her branches are long life.

text.KJV: 1:22 A furious man cannot be iustified, for the sway of his fury shalbe his destruction.
text.NRSV: Unjust anger cannot be justified, for anger tips the scale to one’s ruin.

text.KJV: 1:26 If thou desire wisedome, keepe the commaundements, and the Lord shall giue her vnto thee.
text.NRSV: If you desire wisdom, keep the commandments, and the Lord will lavish her upon you.

text.KJV: 1:27 For the feare of the Lord is wisdome, and instruction: and faith and meekenesse are his delight.
text.NRSV: For the fear of the Lord is wisdom and discipline; faithfulness and humility are his delight.

text.KJV: 1:28 Distrust not the feare of the Lord when thou art poore: and come not vnto him with a double heart.
text.NRSV: Do not disobey the fear of the Lord; do not approach him with a divided mind.

text.KJV: 1:30 Exalt not thy selfe, lest thou fall, and bring dishonor vpon thy soule, and so God discouer thy secrets, and cast thee downe in the midst of the congregation, because thou camest not in trueth, to the feare of the Lord: but thy heart is full of deceit.
text.NRSV: Do not exalt yourself, or you may fall and bring dishonor upon yourself.

text.KJV: 2:1 My sonne, if thou come to serue the Lorde, prepare thy soule for temptation.
text.NABRE: 2:1 Chapter 2 Trust in God - My child, when you come to serve the Lord, prepare yourself for trials.
text.NRSV: My child, when you come to serve the Lord, prepare yourself for testing.

text.KJV: 2:2 Set thy heart aright, and constantly endure, and make not haste in time of trouble.
text.NABRE: 2:2 Be sincere of heart and steadfast, and do not be impetuous in time of adversity.
text.NRSV: Set your heart right and be steadfast, and do not be impetuous in time of calamity.

text.KJV: 2:3 Cleaue vnto him, and depart not away, that thou mayest be increased at thy last end.
text.NABRE: 2:3 Cling to him, do not leave him, that you may prosper in your last days.
text.NRSV: Cling to him and do not depart, so that your last days may be prosperous.

text.KJV: 2:4 Whatsoeuer is brought vpon thee, take cheerefully, and bee patient when thou art changed to a lowe estate.
text.NABRE: 2:4 Accept whatever happens to you; in periods of humiliation be patient.
text.NRSV: Accept whatever befalls you, and in times of humiliation be patient.

text.KJV: 2:5 For gold is tried in the fire, and acceptable men in the furnace of aduersitie.
text.NABRE: 2:5 For in fire gold is tested, and the chosen, in the crucible of humiliation.
text.NRSV: For gold is tested in the fire, and those found acceptable, in the furnace of humiliation.

text.KJV: 2:6 Beleeue in him, and he will helpe thee, order thy way aright, and trust in him.
text.NABRE: 2:6 Trust in God, and he will help you; make your ways straight and hope in him.
text.NRSV: Trust in him, and he will help you; make your ways straight, and hope in him.

text.KJV: 2:7 Ye that feare the Lord, waite for his mercie, and goe not aside, lest ye fall.
text.NABRE: 2:7 You that fear the Lord, wait for his mercy, do not stray lest you fall.
text.NRSV: You who fear the Lord, wait for his mercy; do not stray, or you may fall.

text.KJV: 2:8 Yee that feare the Lord, beleeue him, and your reward shall not faile.
text.NABRE: 2:8 You that fear the Lord, trust in him, and your reward will not be lost.
text.NRSV: You who fear the Lord, trust in him, and your reward will not be lost.

text.KJV: 2:9 Ye that feare the Lord, hope for good, and for euerlasting ioy and mercy.
text.NABRE: 2:9 You that fear the Lord, hope for good things, for lasting joy and mercy.
text.NRSV: You who fear the Lord, hope for good things, for lasting joy and mercy.

text.KJV: 2:10 Looke at the generations of old, and see, did euer any trust in the Lord, and was confounded? or did any abide in his feare, & was forsaken? or whom did hee euer despise, that called vpon him?
text.NABRE: 2:10 Consider the generations long past and see: has anyone trusted in the Lord and been disappointed? Has anyone persevered in his fear and been forsaken? has anyone called upon him and been ignored?
text.NRSV: Consider the ancient generations and see: who ever trusted in the Lord and was put to shame? Or who ever persevered in the fear of the Lord and was forsaken? Or who ever called upon him and was overlooked?

text.KJV: 2:11 For the Lord is full of compassion, and mercie, longsuffering, and very pitifull, and forgiueth sinnes, and saueth in time of affliction.
text.NABRE: 2:11 For the Lord is compassionate and merciful; forgives sins and saves in time of trouble.
text.NRSV: For the Lord is compassionate and merciful; he forgives sins and saves in time of distress.

text.KJV: 2:12 Woe be to fearefull hearts, and faint hands, and the sinner that goeth two wayes.
text.NABRE: 2:12 Woe to timid hearts and drooping hands, to the sinner who walks a double path!
text.NRSV: Woe to timid hearts and to slack hands, and to the sinner who walks a double path!

text.KJV: 2:13 Woe vnto him that is faint hearted, for he beleeueth not, therefore shall he not be defended.
text.NABRE: 2:13 Woe to the faint of heart! For they do not trust, and therefore have no shelter!
text.NRSV: Woe to the fainthearted who have no trust! Therefore they will have no shelter.

text.KJV: 2:14 Woe vnto you that haue lost patience: and what will ye doe when the Lord shall visite you?
text.NABRE: 2:14 Woe to you that have lost hope! what will you do at the Lord’s visitation?
text.NRSV: Woe to you who have lost your nerve! What will you do when the Lord’s reckoning comes?

text.KJV: 2:15 They that feare the Lord, will not disobey his word, and they that loue him, will keepe his wayes.
text.NABRE: 2:15 Those who fear the Lord do not disobey his words; those who love him keep his ways.
text.NRSV: Those who fear the Lord do not disobey his words, and those who love him keep his ways.

text.KJV: 2:17 They that feare the Lord, will prepare their hearts, and humble their soules in his sight:
text.NABRE: 2:17 Those who fear the Lord prepare their hearts and humble themselves before him.
text.NRSV: Those who fear the Lord prepare their hearts, and humble themselves before him.

text.KJV: 2:18 Saying, We will fal into the hands of the Lord, and not into the hands of men: for as his maiestie is, so is his mercie.
text.NABRE: 2:18 Let us fall into the hands of the Lord and not into the hands of mortals, For equal to his majesty is his mercy; and equal to his name are his works.
text.NRSV: Let us fall into the hands of the Lord, but not into the hands of mortals; for equal to his majesty is his mercy, and equal to his name are his works.

text.KJV: 3:1 Heare mee your father, O children, and doe thereafter, that ye may be safe.
text.NABRE: 3:1 Chapter 3 Responsibilities to Parents - Children, listen to me, your father; act accordingly, that you may be safe.
text.NRSV: Listen to me your father, O children; act accordingly, that you may be kept in safety.

text.KJV: 3:2 For the Lord hath giuen the father honour ouer the children, and hath confirmed the authoritie of the mother ouer the sonnes.
text.NABRE: 3:2 For the Lord sets a father in honor over his children and confirms a mother’s authority over her sons.
text.NRSV: For the Lord honors a father above his children, and he confirms a mother’s right over her sons.

text.KJV: 3:3 Who so honoureth his father, maketh an atonement for his sinnes.
text.NABRE: 3:3 Those who honor their father atone for sins;
text.NRSV: Those who honor their father atone for sins,

text.KJV: 3:4 And he that honoureth his mother, is as one that layeth vp treasure.
text.NABRE: 3:4 they store up riches who respect their mother.
text.NRSV: and those who respect their mother are like those who lay up treasure.

text.KJV: 3:8 Honour thy father and mother, both in word and deed, that a blessing may come vpon thee from them.
text.NABRE: 3:8 In word and deed honor your father, that all blessings may come to you.
text.NRSV: Honor your father by word and deed, that his blessing may come upon you.

text.KJV: 3:9 For the blessing of the father establisheth the houses of children, but the curse of the mother rooteth out foundations.
text.NABRE: 3:9 A father’s blessing gives a person firm roots, but a mother’s curse uproots the growing plant.
text.NRSV: For a father’s blessing strengthens the houses of the children, but a mother’s curse uproots their foundations.

text.KJV: 3:12 My sonne, helpe thy father in his age, and grieue him not as long as hee liueth.
text.NABRE: 3:12 My son, be steadfast in honoring your father; do not grieve him as long as he lives.
text.NRSV: My child, help your father in his old age, and do not grieve him as long as he lives;

text.KJV: 3:13 And if his vnderstanding faile, haue patience with him, and despise him not, when thou art in thy ful strength.
text.NABRE: 3:13 Even if his mind fails, be considerate of him; do not revile him because you are in your prime.
text.NRSV: even if his mind fails, be patient with him; because you have all your faculties do not despise him.

text.KJV: 3:17 My sonne, goe on with thy businesse in meekenesse, so shalt thou be beloued of him that is approued.
text.NABRE: 3:17 Humility - My son, conduct your affairs with humility, and you will be loved more than a giver of gifts.
text.NRSV: My child, perform your tasks with humility; then you will be loved by those whom God accepts.

text.KJV: 3:18 The greater thou art, the more humble thy selfe, and thou shalt find fauour before the Lord.
text.NABRE: 3:18 Humble yourself the more, the greater you are, and you will find mercy in the sight of God.
text.NRSV: The greater you are, the more you must humble yourself; so you will find favor in the sight of the Lord.

text.KJV: 3:21 Seeke not out the things that are too hard for thee, neither search the things that are aboue thy strength.
text.NRSV: Neither seek what is too difficult for you, nor investigate what is beyond your power.

text.KJV: 3:22 But what is commaunded thee, thinke thereupon with reuerence, for it is not needfull for thee, to see with thine eyes, the things that are in secret.
text.NRSV: Reflect upon what has been assigned to you, for you do not need what is hidden.

text.KJV: 3:26 A stubborne heart shall fare euill at the last, and he that loueth danger shall perish therein.
text.NRSV: A stubborn mind will fare badly at the end, and whoever loves danger will perish in it.

text.KJV: 3:30 Water will quench a flaming fire, and almes maketh an attonement for sinnes.
text.NRSV: As water extinguishes a blazing fire, so almsgiving atones for sin.

text.KJV: 4:1 My sonne, defraude not the poore of his liuing, and make not the needy eies to waite long.
text.NABRE: 4:1 Chapter 4 - My child, do not mock the life of the poor; do not keep needy eyes waiting.
text.NRSV: My child, do not cheat the poor of their living, and do not keep needy eyes waiting.

text.KJV: 4:2 Make not an hungry soule sorrowfull, neither prouoke a man in his distresse.
text.NABRE: 4:2 Do not grieve the hungry, nor anger the needy.
text.NRSV: Do not grieve the hungry, or anger one in need.

text.KJV: 4:6 For if he curse thee in the bitternesse of his soule, his prayer shall be heard of him that made him.
text.NABRE: 4:6 If in their pain they cry out bitterly, their Rock will hear the sound of their cry.
text.NRSV: For if in bitterness of soul some should curse you, their Creator will hear their prayer.

text.KJV: 4:7 Get thy selfe the loue of the congregation, and bow thy head to a great man.
text.NABRE: 4:7 Social Conduct - Endear yourself to the assembly; before the city’s ruler bow your head.
text.NRSV: Endear yourself to the congregation; bow your head low to the great.

text.KJV: 4:8 Let it not grieue thee to bowe downe thine eare to the poore, and giue him a friendly answere with meekenesse.
text.NABRE: 4:8 Give a hearing to the poor, and return their greeting with deference;
text.NRSV: Give a hearing to the poor, and return their greeting politely.

text.KJV: 4:10 Be as a father vnto the fatherlesse, and in stead of a husband vnto their mother, so shalt thou be as the sonne of the most high, and he shall loue thee more then thy mother doeth.
text.NABRE: 4:10 Be like a father to orphans, and take the place of a husband to widows. Then God will call you his child, and he will be merciful to you and deliver you from the pit.
text.NRSV: Be like a father to orphans, and like a husband to their mother; you will then be like a son of the Most High, and he will love you more than your mother does.

text.KJV: 4:11 Wisedome exalteth her children, and layeth hold of them that seeke her.
text.NABRE: 4:11 The Rewards of Wisdom - Wisdom teaches her children and admonishes all who can understand her.
text.NRSV: Wisdom teaches her children and gives help to those who seek her.

text.KJV: 4:12 He that loueth her, loueth life, and they that seeke to her earely, shall be filled with ioy.
text.NABRE: 4:12 Those who love her love life; those who seek her out win the LORD’s favor.
text.NRSV: Whoever loves her loves life, and those who seek her from early morning are filled with joy.

text.KJV: 4:17 For at the first she will walke with him by crooked wayes, and bring feare and dread vpon him, and torment him with her discipline, vntill she may trust his soule, and try him by her Lawes.
text.NABRE: 4:17 “I will walk with them in disguise, and at first I will test them with trials. Fear and dread I will bring upon them and I will discipline them with my constraints. When their hearts are fully with me,
text.NRSV: For at first she will walk with them on tortuous paths; she will bring fear and dread upon them, and will torment them by her discipline until she trusts them.

text.KJV: 4:20 Obserue the opportunitie, and beware of euill, and be not ashamed when it concerneth thy soule.
text.NABRE: 4:20 Sincerity and Justice - My son, watch for the right time; fear what is evil; do not bring shame upon yourself.
text.NRSV: Watch for the opportune time, and beware of evil, and do not be ashamed to be yourself.

text.KJV: 4:21 For there is a shame that bringeth sinne, and there is a shame which is glorie and grace.
text.NABRE: 4:21 There is a shame heavy with guilt, and a shame that brings glory and respect.
text.NRSV: For there is a shame that leads to sin, and there is a shame that is glory and favor.

text.KJV: 4:26 Bee not ashamed to confesse thy sinnes, and force not the course of the riuer.
text.NABRE: 4:26 Do not be ashamed to acknowledge your sins, and do not struggle against a rushing stream.
text.NRSV: Do not be ashamed to confess your sins, and do not try to stop the current of a river.

text.KJV: 4:28 Striue for the trueth vnto death, and the Lord shall fight for thee.
text.NABRE: 4:28 Even to the death, fight for what is right, and the LORD will do battle for you.
text.NRSV: Fight to the death for truth, and the Lord God will fight for you.

text.KJV: 5:1 Set not thy heart vpon thy goods, and say not, I haue ynough for my life.
text.NABRE: 5:1 Chapter 5 Against Presumption - Do not rely on your wealth, or say, “I have the power.”
text.NRSV: Do not rely on your wealth, or say, “I have enough.”

text.KJV: 5:2 Folow not thine owne minde, and thy strength, to walke in the wayes of thy heart:
text.NABRE: 5:2 Do not rely on your strength in following the desires of your heart.
text.NRSV: Do not follow your inclination and strength in pursuing the desires of your heart.

text.KJV: 5:4 Say not, I haue sinned, and what harme hath happened vnto mee? for the Lord is long suffering, he wil in no wise let thee goe.
text.NABRE: 5:4 Do not say, “I have sinned, yet what has happened to me?” for the LORD is slow to anger!
text.NRSV: Do not say, “I sinned, yet what has happened to me?” for the Lord is slow to anger.

text.KJV: 5:5 Concerning propitiation, bee not without feare to adde sinne vnto sinne.
text.NABRE: 5:5 Do not be so confident of forgiveness that you add sin upon sin.
text.NRSV: Do not be so confident of forgiveness that you add sin to sin.

text.KJV: 5:7 Make no tarying to turne to the Lord, and put not off from day to day: for suddenly shal the wrath of the Lord come foorth, and in thy securitie thou shalt be destroyed, and perish in the day of vengeance.
text.NABRE: 5:7 Do not delay turning back to the LORD, do not put it off day after day. For suddenly his wrath will come forth; at the time of vengeance, you will perish.
text.NRSV: Do not delay to return to the Lord, and do not postpone it from day to day;

text.KJV: 5:8 Set not thy heart vpon goods vniustly gotten: for they shall not profit thee in the day of calamitie.
text.NABRE: 5:8 Do not rely on deceitful wealth, for it will be no help on the day of wrath.
text.NRSV: for suddenly the wrath of the Lord will come upon you, and at the time of punishment you will perish.

text.KJV: 5:10 Be stedfast in thy vnderstanding, and let thy word be the same.
text.NABRE: 5:10 Be steadfast regarding your knowledge, and let your speech be consistent.
text.NRSV: Be steadfast in your understanding, and let your speech be consistent.

text.KJV: 5:11 Be swift to heare, and let thy life be sincere, & with patience giue answere.
text.NABRE: 5:11 Be swift to hear, but slow to answer.
text.NRSV: Be quick to hear, but be deliberate in answering.

text.KJV: 5:12 If thou hast vnderstanding, answer thy neighbour, if not, lay thy hand vpon thy mouth.
text.NABRE: 5:12 If you can, answer your neighbor; if not, place your hand over your mouth!
text.NRSV: If you know what to say, answer your neighbor; but if not, put your hand over your mouth.

text.KJV: 5:13 Honour and shame is in talke; and the tongue of man is his fall.
text.NABRE: 5:13 Honor and dishonor through speaking! The tongue can be your downfall.
text.NRSV: Honor and dishonor come from speaking, and the tongue of mortals may be their downfall.

text.KJV: 6:5 Sweet language will multiply friends: and a faire speaking tongue will increase kinde greetings.
text.NABRE: 6:5 True Friendship - Pleasant speech multiplies friends, and gracious lips, friendly greetings.
text.NRSV: Pleasant speech multiplies friends, and a gracious tongue multiplies courtesies.

text.KJV: 6:6 Be in peace with many: neuerthelesse haue but one counseller of a thousand.
text.NABRE: 6:6 Let those who are friendly to you be many, but one in a thousand your confidant.
text.NRSV: Let those who are at peace with you be many, but let your advisers be one in a thousand.

text.KJV: 6:7 If thou wouldst get a friend, proue him first, and be not hasty to credit him.
text.NABRE: 6:7 When you gain friends, gain them through testing, and do not be quick to trust them.
text.NRSV: When you gain friends, gain them through testing, and do not trust them hastily.

text.KJV: 6:8 For some man is a friend for his owne occasion, and will not abide in the day of thy trouble.
text.NABRE: 6:8 For there are friends when it suits them, but they will not be around in time of trouble.
text.NRSV: For there are friends who are such when it suits them, but they will not stand by you in time of trouble.

text.KJV: 6:13 Separate thy selfe from thine enemies, and take heed of thy friends.
text.NABRE: 6:13 Stay away from your enemies, and be on guard with your friends.
text.NRSV: Separate yourself from your enemies, and be on the watch where your friends are concerned.

text.KJV: 6:14 A faithfull friend is a strong defence: and hee that hath found such an one, hath found a treasure.
text.NABRE: 6:14 Faithful friends are a sturdy shelter; whoever finds one finds a treasure.
text.NRSV: Faithful friends are a sturdy shelter; whoever finds one has found a treasure.

text.KJV: 6:15 Nothing doeth counteruaile a faithful friend, and his excellencie is vnualuable.
text.NABRE: 6:15 Faithful friends are beyond price, no amount can balance their worth.
text.NRSV: Faithful friends are beyond price; no amount can be weighed out against their worth.

text.KJV: 6:16 A faithfull friend is the medicine of life, and they that feare the Lord shal finde him.
text.NABRE: 6:16 Faithful friends are life-saving medicine; those who fear God will find them.
text.NRSV: Faithful friends are life-saving medicine; and those who fear the Lord will find them.

text.KJV: 6:18 My sonne, gather instruction from thy youth vp: so shalt thou finde wisedome till thine old age.
text.NABRE: 6:18 Blessings of Wisdom - My child, from your youth choose discipline; and when you have gray hair you will find wisdom.
text.NRSV: My child, from your youth choose discipline, and when you have gray hair you will still find wisdom.

text.KJV: 6:19 Come vnto her as one that ploweth, and soweth, and wait for her good fruits, for thou shalt not toile much in labouring about her, but thou shalt eat of her fruits right soone.
text.NABRE: 6:19 As though plowing and sowing, draw close to her; then wait for her bountiful crops. For in cultivating her you will work but little, and soon you will eat her fruit.
text.NRSV: Come to her like one who plows and sows, and wait for her good harvest.

text.KJV: 6:27 Search and seeke, and shee shall bee made knowen vnto thee, and when thou hast got hold of her, let her not goe.
text.NABRE: 6:27 Inquire and search, seek and find; when you get hold of her, do not let her go.
text.NRSV: Search her out and seek her, and she will become known to you; and when you get hold of her, do not let her go.

text.KJV: 6:28 For at the last thou shalt finde her rest, and that shalbe turned to thy ioy.
text.NABRE: 6:28 Thus at last you will find rest in her, and she will become your joy.
text.NRSV: For at last you will find the rest she gives, and she will be changed into joy for you.

text.KJV: 6:36 And if thou seest a man of vnderstanding, get thee betimes vnto him, and let thy foote weare the steps of his doore.
text.NABRE: 6:36 If you see the intelligent, seek them out; let your feet wear away their doorsteps!
text.NRSV: If you see an intelligent person, rise early to visit him; let your foot wear out his doorstep.

text.KJV: 6:37 Let thy minde be vpon the ordinances of the Lord, & meditate continually in his commandements: he shal establish thine heart, and giue thee wisedome at thine owne desire.
text.NABRE: 6:37 Reflect on the law of the Most High, and let his commandments be your constant study. Then he will enlighten your mind, and make you wise as you desire.
text.NRSV: Reflect on the statutes of the Lord, and meditate at all times on his commandments. It is he who will give insight to your mind, and your desire for wisdom will be granted.

text.KJV: 7:1 Doe no euill, so shall no harme come vnto thee.
text.NABRE: 7:1 Chapter 7 Conduct Toward God and Neighbor - Do no evil, and evil will not overtake you;
text.NRSV: Do no evil, and evil will never overtake you.

text.KJV: 7:2 Depart from the vniust, and iniquitie shall turne away from thee.
text.NABRE: 7:2 avoid wickedness, and it will turn away from you.
text.NRSV: Stay away from wrong, and it will turn away from you.

text.KJV: 7:3 My sonne, sow not vpon the furrowes of vnrighteousnesse, and thou shalt not reape them seuen folde.
text.NABRE: 7:3 Do not sow in the furrows of injustice, lest you harvest it sevenfold.
text.NRSV: Do not sow in the furrows of injustice, or you may reap a sevenfold harvest.

text.KJV: 7:6 Seeke not to be iudge, being not able to take away iniquitie, lest at any time thou feare the person of the mightie, and lay a stumbling blocke in the way of thy vprightnesse.
text.NABRE: 7:6 Do not seek to become a judge if you do not have the strength to root out crime, Lest you show fear in the presence of the prominent and mar your integrity.
text.NRSV: Do not seek to become a judge, or you may be unable to root out injustice;

text.KJV: 7:8 Bind not one sinne vpon another, for in one thou shalt not be vnpunished.
text.NABRE: 7:8 Do not plot to repeat a sin; even for one, you will not go unpunished.
text.NRSV: Do not commit a sin twice, for not even once will you go unpunished.

text.KJV: 7:10 Be not faint hearted when thou makest thy prayer, and neglect not to giue almes.
text.NABRE: 7:10 Do not be impatient in prayer or neglect almsgiving.
text.NRSV: Do not grow weary in prayer; do not neglect to give alms.

text.KJV: 7:11 Laugh no man to scorne in the bitternesse of his soule: for there is one which humbleth and exalteth.
text.NABRE: 7:11 Do not ridicule the embittered; Remember: there is One who exalts and humbles.
text.NRSV: Do not ridicule a person who is embittered in spirit, for there is One who humbles and exalts.

text.KJV: 7:14 Use not many words in a multitude of Elders, and make not much babling when thou prayest.
text.NABRE: 7:14 Do not babble in the assembly of the elders or repeat the words of your prayer.
text.NRSV: Do not prattle in the assembly of the elders, and do not repeat yourself in your prayer.

text.KJV: 7:15 Hate not laborious worke, neither husbandrie, which the most High hath ordeined.
text.NABRE: 7:15 Do not hate hard work; work was assigned by God.
text.NRSV: Do not hate hard-working labor or farm work, which was created by the Most High.

text.KJV: 7:18 Change not a friend for any good by no meanes: neither a faithfull brother for the gold of Ophir.
text.NABRE: 7:18 Duties of Family Life, Religion and Charity - Do not barter a friend for money, or a true brother for the gold of Ophir.
text.NRSV: Do not exchange a friend for money, or a real brother for the gold of Ophir.

text.KJV: 7:19 Forgoe not a wise and good woman: for her grace is aboue gold.
text.NABRE: 7:19 Do not reject a sensible wife; a gracious wife is more precious than pearls.
text.NRSV: Do not miss out on a wise and good wife, for her charm is worth more than gold.

text.KJV: 7:21 Let thy soule loue a good seruant, and defraud him not of liberty.
text.NABRE: 7:21 Love wise servants as yourself; do not refuse them freedom.
text.NRSV: Love a wise slave like yourself; do not withhold from him his freedom.

text.KJV: 7:23 Hast thou children? instruct them, and bow downe their necke from their youth.
text.NABRE: 7:23 Do you have sons? Correct them and cure their stubbornness in their early youth.
text.NRSV: If you have children, discipline them, and make them obedient from their youth.

text.KJV: 7:27 Honour thy father with thy whole heart, and forget not the sorrowes of thy mother.
text.NABRE: 7:27 With your whole heart honor your father; your mother’s birth pangs do not forget.
text.NRSV: With all your heart honor your father, and do not forget the birth pangs of your mother.

text.KJV: 7:28 Remember that thou wast begot of them, and how canst thou recompense them the things that they haue done for thee?
text.NABRE: 7:28 Remember, of these parents you were born; what can you give them for all they gave you?
text.NRSV: Remember that it was of your parents you were born; how can you repay what they have given to you?

text.KJV: 7:32 And stretch thine hand vnto the poore, that thy blessing may be perfected
text.NABRE: 7:32 To the poor also extend your hand, that your blessing may be complete.
text.NRSV: Stretch out your hand to the poor, so that your blessing may be complete.

text.KJV: 7:33 A gift hath grace in the sight of euery man liuing, and for the dead deteine it not.
text.NABRE: 7:33 Give your gift to all the living, and do not withhold your kindness from the dead.
text.NRSV: Give graciously to all the living; do not withhold your kindness even from the dead.

text.KJV: 7:34 Faile not to bee with them that weepe, and mourne with them that mourne.
text.NABRE: 7:34 Do not avoid those who weep, but mourn with those who mourn.
text.NRSV: Do not avoid those who weep, but mourn with those who mourn.

text.KJV: 7:35 Be not slow to visit the sicke: for that shall make thee to be beloued.
text.NABRE: 7:35 Do not hesitate to visit the sick, because for such things you will be loved.
text.NRSV: Do not hesitate to visit the sick, because for such deeds you will be loved.

text.KJV: 7:36 Whatsoeuer thou takest in hand, remember the end, and thou shalt neuer doe amisse.
text.NABRE: 7:36 In whatever you do, remember your last days, and you will never sin.
text.NRSV: In all you do, remember the end of your life, and then you will never sin.

text.KJV: 8:1 Striue not with a mighty man, lest thou fall into his hands.
text.NABRE: 8:1 Chapter 8 Prudence in Dealing with Others - Do not contend with the mighty, lest you fall into their power.
text.NRSV: Do not contend with a powerful person, or you may fall into his hands.

text.KJV: 8:2 Bee not at variance with a rich man, lest he ouerweigh thee: for gold hath destroyed many, and peruerted the hearts of kings.
text.NABRE: 8:2 Do not quarrel with the rich, lest they pay out the price of your downfall. For gold has unsettled many, and wealth perverts the character of princes.
text.NRSV: Do not quarrel with the rich, in case their resources outweigh yours; for gold has ruined many, and has perverted the minds of kings.

text.KJV: 8:3 Striue not with a man that is full of tongue, and heape not wood vpon his fire.
text.NABRE: 8:3 Do not quarrel with loud-mouths, or heap wood upon their fire.
text.NRSV: Do not argue with a loud-mouthed person, and do not heap wood on his fire.

text.KJV: 8:5 Reproch not a man that turneth from sinne, but remember that we are all worthy of punishment.
text.NABRE: 8:5 Do not reproach one who turns away from sin; remember, we all are guilty.
text.NRSV: Do not reproach one who is turning away from sin; remember that we all are deserving of punishment.

text.KJV: 8:6 Dishonour not a man in his old age: for euen some of vs waxe old.
text.NABRE: 8:6 Do not insult one who is old, for some of us will also grow old.
text.NRSV: Do not dishonor an old person, for some of us are growing old.

text.KJV: 8:7 Reioice not ouer thy greatest enemie being dead, but remember that we die all.
text.NABRE: 8:7 Do not rejoice when someone dies; remember, we are all to be gathered in.
text.NRSV: Do not rejoice over anyone’s death; remember that we all must die.

text.KJV: 8:8 Despise not the discourse of the wise, but acquaint thy selfe with their prouerbs; for of them thou shalt learne instruction, & how to serue great men with ease.
text.NABRE: 8:8 Do not neglect the discourse of the wise, but busy yourself with their proverbs; For in this way you will acquire the training to stand in the presence of princes.
text.NRSV: Do not slight the discourse of the sages, but busy yourself with their maxims; because from them you will learn discipline and how to serve princes.

text.KJV: 8:9 Misse not the discourse of the Elders: for they also learned of their fathers, and of them thou shalt learne vnderstanding, and to giue answere as need requireth.
text.NABRE: 8:9 Do not reject the tradition of the elders which they have heard from their ancestors; For from it you will learn how to answer when the need arises.
text.NRSV: Do not reject the tradition of the elders, which they learned from their ancestors; for from it you will learn how to understand and how to give an answer in time of need.

text.KJV: 8:10 Kindle not the coales of a sinner, lest thou be burnt with the flame of his fire.
text.NABRE: 8:10 Do not kindle the coals of sinners, lest you be burned in their flaming fire.
text.NRSV: Do not kindle the coals of a sinner, or you may be burned in his flaming fire.

text.KJV: 8:15 Trauaile not by the way with a bold fellow, least he become grieuous vnto thee: for he will doe according to his owne will, and thou shalt perish with him through his folly.
text.NABRE: 8:15 Do not travel with the ruthless lest they weigh you down with calamity; For they will only go their own way, and through their folly you will also perish.
text.NRSV: Do not travel with a reckless person, or he will be a burden to you; for he will do as he pleases, and through his folly you will perish with him.

text.KJV: 9:1 Be not iealous ouer the wife of thy bosome, and teach her not an euil lesson against thy selfe.
text.NABRE: 9:1 Chapter 9 Advice Concerning Women - Do not be jealous of the wife of your bosom, lest you teach her to do evil against you.
text.NRSV: Do not be jealous of the wife of your bosom, or you will teach her an evil lesson to your own hurt.

text.KJV: 9:2 Giue not thy soule vnto a woman, to set her foot vpon thy substance.
text.NABRE: 9:2 Do not give a woman power over you to trample on your dignity.
text.NRSV: Do not give yourself to a woman and let her trample down your strength.

text.KJV: 9:3 Meete not with an harlot, least thou fall into her snares.
text.NABRE: 9:3 Do not go near a strange woman, lest you fall into her snares.
text.NRSV: Do not go near a loose woman, or you will fall into her snares.

text.KJV: 9:8 Turne away thine eye from a beautifull woman, and looke not vpon anothers beautie: for many haue beene deceiued by the beautie of a woman, for heerewith loue is kindled as a fire.
text.NABRE: 9:8 Avert your eyes from a shapely woman; do not gaze upon beauty that is not yours; Through woman’s beauty many have been ruined, for love of it burns like fire.
text.NRSV: Turn away your eyes from a shapely woman, and do not gaze at beauty belonging to another; many have been seduced by a woman’s beauty, and by it passion is kindled like a fire.

text.KJV: 9:10 Forsake not an old friend, for the new is not comparable to him: a new friend is as new wine: when it is old, thou shalt drinke it with pleasure.
text.NABRE: 9:10 Choice of Friends - Do not abandon old friends; new ones cannot equal them. A new friend is like new wine— when it has aged, you drink it with pleasure.
text.NRSV: Do not abandon old friends, for new ones cannot equal them. A new friend is like new wine; when it has aged, you can drink it with pleasure.

text.KJV: 9:11 Enuy not the glory of a sinner: for thou knowest not what shall be his end.
text.NABRE: 9:11 Do not envy the wicked for you do not know when their day will come.
text.NRSV: Do not envy the success of sinners, for you do not know what their end will be.

text.KJV: 9:13 Keepe thee farre from the man that hath power to kill, so shalt thou not doubt the feare of death: and if thou come vnto him, make no fault, least he take away thy life presently: remember that thou goest in the midst of snares, and that thou walkest vpon the battlements of the citie.
text.NABRE: 9:13 Keep away from those who have power to kill, and you will not be filled with the dread of death. But if you come near them, do not offend them, lest they take away your life. Know that you are stepping among snares and walking over a net.
text.NRSV: Keep far from those who have power to kill, and you will not be haunted by the fear of death.

text.KJV: 9:15 Let thy talke be with the wise, and all thy communication in the law of the most high.
text.NABRE: 9:15 With the learned exchange ideas; and let all your conversation be about the law of the Most High.
text.NRSV: Reason with the intelligent, and let all your argument be about the law of the Most High.

text.KJV: 9:17 For the hand of the artificer, the worke shall be commended: and the wise ruler of the people, for his speech.
text.NABRE: 9:17 Concerning Rulers - Work by skilled hands will earn praise; but the people’s leader is proved wise by his words.
text.NRSV: A work is praised for the skill of the artisan, so a people’s leader is proved wise by his words.

text.KJV: 10:1 A wise iudge will instruct his people, & the gouernement of a prudent man is well ordered.
text.NABRE: 10:1 Chapter 10 - A wise magistrate gives stability to his people, and government by the intelligent is well ordered.
text.NRSV: A wise magistrate educates his people, and the government of an intelligent person is well ordered.

text.KJV: 10:2 As the iudge of the people is himselfe, so are his officers, and what maner of man the ruler of the citie is, such are all they that dwell therein.
text.NABRE: 10:2 As the people’s judge, so the officials; as the head of a city, so the inhabitants.
text.NRSV: As the people’s judge is, so are his officials; and as the ruler of the city is, so are all its inhabitants.

text.KJV: 10:3 An vnwise king destroyeth his people, but through the prudence of them which are in authoritie, the citie shalbe inhabited.
text.NABRE: 10:3 A reckless king destroys his people, but a city grows through the intelligence of its princes.
text.NRSV: An untrained king ruins his people, but a city prospers through the intelligence of its rulers.

text.KJV: 10:4 The power of the earth is in the hand of the Lord, and in due time hee will set ouer it one that is profitable.
text.NABRE: 10:4 Sovereignty over the earth is in the hand of God, who appoints the right person for the right time.
text.NRSV: The government of the earth is in the hand of the Lord, and over it he will raise up the right leader for the time.

text.KJV: 10:7 Pride is hatefull before God, and man: and by both doeth one commit iniquitie.
text.NABRE: 10:7 Odious to the Lord and to mortals is pride, and for both oppression is a crime.
text.NRSV: Arrogance is hateful to the Lord and to mortals, and injustice is outrageous to both.

text.KJV: 10:8 Because of vnrighteous dealings, iniuries, and riches got by deceit, the kingdome is translated from one people to another.
text.NABRE: 10:8 Sovereignty is transferred from one people to another because of the lawlessness of the proud.
text.NRSV: Sovereignty passes from nation to nation because of injustice and insolence and wealth.

text.KJV: 10:9 Why is earth and ashes proude? There is not a more wicked thing, then a couetous man: for such an one setteth his owne soule to sale, because while he liueth, he casteth away his bowels.
text.NABRE: 10:9 Why are dust and ashes proud? Even during life the body decays.
text.NRSV: How can dust and ashes be proud? Even in life the human body decays.

text.KJV: 10:12 The beginning of pride is, when one departeth from God, and his heart is turned away from his maker.
text.NABRE: 10:12 The beginning of pride is stubbornness in withdrawing the heart from one’s Maker.
text.NRSV: The beginning of human pride is to forsake the Lord; the heart has withdrawn from its Maker.

text.KJV: 10:14 The Lord hath cast downe the thrones of proud Princes, and set vp the meeke in their stead.
text.NABRE: 10:14 God overturns the thrones of the proud and enthrones the lowly in their place.
text.NRSV: The Lord overthrows the thrones of rulers, and enthrones the lowly in their place.

text.KJV: 10:18 Pride was not made for men, nor furious anger for them that are borne of a woman.
text.NABRE: 10:18 Insolence does not befit mortals, nor impudent anger those born of women.
text.NRSV: Pride was not created for human beings, or violent anger for those born of women.

text.KJV: 10:24 Great men, and Iudges, and Potentates shall bee honoured, yet is there none of them greater then he that feareth the Lord.
text.NRSV: The great, the judge, and the ruler are honored, but none of them is greater than the one who fears the Lord.

text.KJV: 10:27 Better is he that laboureth and aboundeth in all things, then hee that boasteth himselfe, and wanteth bread.
text.NRSV: Better are those who work and have an abundance of everything, than those who go about boasting of their rank but lack food.

text.KJV: 10:28 My sonne, glorifie thy soule in meekenesse, and giue it honour according to the dignitie thereof.
text.NRSV: My child, honor yourself with humility, and give yourself the esteem you deserve.

text.KJV: 11:1 Wisedome lifteth vp the head of him that is of low degree, and maketh him to sit among great men.
text.NABRE: 11:1 Chapter 11 - The wisdom of the poor lifts their head high and sets them among princes.
text.NRSV: The wisdom of the lowly lifts up their heads, and seats them among the great.

text.KJV: 11:2 Commend not a man for his beautie, neither abhorre a man for his outward appearance.
text.NABRE: 11:2 Do not praise anyone for good looks; or despise anyone because of appearance.
text.NRSV: Do not praise individuals for their good looks, or loathe anyone because of appearance.

text.KJV: 11:3 The Bee is little among such as flie, but her fruite is the chiefe of sweete things.
text.NABRE: 11:3 The bee is least among winged creatures, but it reaps the choicest of harvests.
text.NRSV: The bee is small among flying creatures, but her product is the best of sweet things.

text.KJV: 11:4 Boast not of thy cloathing and raiment, and exalt not thy selfe in the day of honour: for the workes of the Lord are wonderfull, and his workes among men are hidden.
text.NABRE: 11:4 Do not mock the one who wears only a loin-cloth, or scoff at a person’s bitter day. For strange are the deeds of the LORD, hidden from mortals his work.
text.NRSV: Do not boast about your clothing, or lift yourself up on the day you are honored; for the works of the Lord are wonderful, and his activities are hidden from mortals.

text.KJV: 11:7 Blame not before thou hast examined the trueth: vnderstand first, and then rebuke.
text.NABRE: 11:7 Moderation and Patience - Before investigating, do not find fault; examine first, then criticize.
text.NRSV: Do not find fault before you investigate; examine first, and then criticize.

text.KJV: 11:8 Answere not, before thou hast heard the cause: neither interrupt men in the midst of their talke.
text.NABRE: 11:8 Before listening, do not say a word, interrupt no one in the midst of speaking.
text.NRSV: Do not answer before you listen, and do not interrupt when another is speaking.

text.KJV: 11:9 Striue not in a matter that concerneth thee not: and sit not in iudgement with sinners.
text.NABRE: 11:9 Do not dispute about what is not your concern; in the quarrels of the arrogant do not take part.
text.NRSV: Do not argue about a matter that does not concern you, and do not sit with sinners when they judge a case.

text.KJV: 11:10 My sonne, meddle not with many matters: for if thou meddle much, thou shalt not be innocent: and if thou follow after, thou shalt not obtaine, neither shalt thou escape by flying.
text.NABRE: 11:10 My son, why increase your anxiety, since whoever is greedy for wealth will not be blameless? Even if you chase after it, you will never overtake it; and by fleeing you will not escape.
text.NRSV: My child, do not busy yourself with many matters; if you multiply activities, you will not go unpunished.

text.KJV: 11:14 Prosperitie and aduersitie, life and death, pouerty and riches, come of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 11:14 Good and evil, life and death, poverty and riches—all are from the LORD.
text.NRSV: Good things and bad, life and death, poverty and wealth, come from the Lord.

text.KJV: 11:21 Marueile not at the workes of sinners, but trust in the Lord, and abide in thy labour: for it is an easie thing in the sight of the Lord, on the sudden to make a poore man rich.
text.NRSV: Do not wonder at the works of a sinner, but trust in the Lord and keep at your job; for it is easy in the sight of the Lord to make the poor rich suddenly, in a moment.

text.KJV: 11:26 For it is an easie thing vnto the Lord in the day of death, to reward a man according to his wayes.
text.NRSV: It is easy for the Lord on the day of death to reward individuals according to their conduct.

text.KJV: 11:28 Iudge none blessed before his death: for a man shall bee knowen in his children.
text.NRSV: Call no one happy before his death; by how he ends, a person becomes known.

text.KJV: 12:1 When thou wilt doe good, know to whō thou doest it, so shalt thou be thanked for thy benefites.
text.NABRE: 12:1 Chapter 12 - If you do good, know for whom you are doing it, and your kindness will have its effect.
text.NRSV: If you do good, know to whom you do it, and you will be thanked for your good deeds.

text.KJV: 12:2 Do good to the godly man, and thou shalt find a recompence, and if not from him, yet from the most high.
text.NABRE: 12:2 Do good to the righteous and reward will be yours, if not from them, from the LORD.
text.NRSV: Do good to the devout, and you will be repaid—if not by them, certainly by the Most High.

text.KJV: 12:4 Giue to the godly man, and helpe not a sinner.
text.NABRE: 12:4 Give to the good but refuse the sinner;
text.NRSV: Give to the devout, but do not help the sinner.

text.KJV: 12:8 A friend cannot be knowen in prosperitie, and an enemy cannot be hidden in aduersitie.
text.NRSV: A friend is not known in prosperity, nor is an enemy hidden in adversity.

text.KJV: 12:10 Neuer trust thine enemie: for like as yron rusteth, so is his wickednesse.
text.NRSV: Never trust your enemy, for like corrosion in copper, so is his wickedness.

text.KJV: 12:13 Who will pitie a charmer that is bitten with a serpent, or any such as come nigh wilde beasts?
text.NRSV: Who pities a snake charmer when he is bitten, or all those who go near wild animals?

text.KJV: 12:14 So one that goeth to a sinner, and is defiled with him in his sinnes, who will pitie?
text.NRSV: So no one pities a person who associates with a sinner and becomes involved in his sins.

text.KJV: 12:16 An enemie speaketh sweetly with his lippes, but in his heart he imagineth how to throw thee into a pit: hee will weepe with his eyes, but if he find opportunitie, hee will not be satisfied with blood.
text.NRSV: An enemy has sweetness on his lips, but in his heart he plans to throw you into a pit; an enemy may have tears in his eyes, but if he finds an opportunity he will never have enough of your blood.

text.KJV: 13:1 He that toucheth pitch, shal be defiled therewith, and hee that hath fellowship with a proude man, shall be like vnto him.
text.NABRE: 13:1 Chapter 13 Caution Regarding Associates - Touch pitch and you blacken your hand; associate with scoundrels and you learn their ways.
text.NRSV: Whoever touches pitch will be defiled, and whoever associates with the proud will become like them.

text.KJV: 13:2 Burthen not thy selfe aboue thy power, while thou liuest, and haue no fellowship with one that is mightier, and richer then thy selfe. For how agree the kettle and the earthen pot together? for if the one be smitten against the other, it shall be broken.
text.NABRE: 13:2 Do not lift a weight too heavy for you, or associate with anyone wealthier than you. How can the clay pot go with the metal cauldron? When they knock together, the pot will be smashed:
text.NRSV: Do not lift a weight beyond your strength, or associate with one who is mightier and richer than you.

text.KJV: 13:3 The rich man hath done wrong, and yet he threatneth withall: the poore is wronged, and he must intreat also.
text.NABRE: 13:3 The rich do wrong and boast of it, while the poor are wronged and beg forgiveness.
text.NRSV: A rich person does a wrong, and even adds insults; a poor person suffers a wrong, and must add apologies.

text.KJV: 13:10 Presse thou not vpon him, lest thou be put backe, stand not farre off, lest thou be forgotten.
text.NABRE: 13:10 Do not draw too close, lest you be rebuffed, but do not keep too far away lest you be regarded as an enemy.
text.NRSV: Do not push forward, or you may be repulsed; and do not keep too far away, or you may be forgotten.

text.KJV: 13:15 Euery beast loueth his like, and euery man loueth his neighbour.
text.NRSV: Every creature loves its like, and every person his neighbor.

text.KJV: 13:16 All flesh consorteth according to kind, and a man will cleaue to his like:
text.NRSV: All living beings associate with their own kind, and people stick to those like themselves.

text.KJV: 13:18 What agreement is there betweene the Hyena and a dogge? and what peace betweene the rich and the poore?
text.NRSV: What peace is there between a hyena and a dog? And what peace between the rich and the poor?

text.KJV: 13:19 As the wilde asse is the lyons pray in the wildernesse: so the rich eate vp the poore.
text.NRSV: Wild asses in the wilderness are the prey of lions; likewise the poor are feeding grounds for the rich.

text.KJV: 13:24 Riches are good vnto him that hath no sinne, and pouerty is euill in the mouth of the vngodly.
text.NRSV: Wealth is good if it is free from sin; poverty is evil only in the opinion of the ungodly.

text.KJV: 13:25 The heart of a man changeth his countenance, whether it be for good or euill: and a merry heart maketh a cheerefull countenance.
text.NRSV: The heart changes the face, either for good or for evil.

text.KJV: 14:1 Blessed is the man that hath not slipt with his mouth, and is not pricked with the multitude of sinnes.
text.NABRE: 14:1 Chapter 14 - Happy those whose mouth causes them no grief, those who are not stung by remorse for sin.
text.NRSV: Happy are those who do not blunder with their lips, and need not suffer remorse for sin.

text.KJV: 14:2 Blessed is hee whose conscience hath not condemned him, and who is not fallen from his hope in the Lord.
text.NABRE: 14:2 Happy are those whose conscience does not reproach them, those who have not lost hope.
text.NRSV: Happy are those whose hearts do not condemn them, and who have not given up their hope.

text.KJV: 14:3 Riches are not comely for a niggard: and what should an enuious man doe with money?
text.NABRE: 14:3 The Use of Wealth - Wealth is not appropriate for the mean-spirited; to misers, what use is gold?
text.NRSV: Riches are not appropriate for a small-minded person; and of what use is wealth to a miser?

text.KJV: 14:5 Hee that is euill to himselfe, to whom will he be good? he shall not take pleasure in his goods.
text.NABRE: 14:5 To whom will they be generous that are stingy with themselves and do not enjoy what is their own?
text.NRSV: He who is evil to himself, to whom will he be good? He will not enjoy his own riches.

text.KJV: 14:11 My sonne, according to thy habilitie doe good to thy selfe, and giue the Lord his due offering.
text.NABRE: 14:11 My son, if you have the means, treat yourself well, and enjoy life as best you can.
text.NRSV: My child, if you have anything, do good to yourself, and bring worthy offerings to the Lord.

text.KJV: 14:12 Remember that death will not be long in comming, and that the couenant of the graue is not shewed vnto thee.
text.NABRE: 14:12 Remember that death does not delay, and you have not been told the grave’s appointed time.
text.NRSV: Remember that death does not tarry, and the decree of Hades has not been shown to you.

text.KJV: 14:13 Doe good vnto thy friend before thou die, and according to thy abilitie, stretch out thy hand and giue to him.
text.NABRE: 14:13 Before you die, be good to your friends; give them a share in what you possess.
text.NRSV: Do good to friends before you die, and reach out and give to them as much as you can.

text.KJV: 14:17 All flesh waxeth old as a garment: for the couenant from the beginning is; thou shalt die the death.
text.NABRE: 14:17 All flesh grows old like a garment; the age-old law is: everyone must die.
text.NRSV: All living beings grow old like a garment; for the decree from of old is, “You must surely die!”

text.KJV: 14:18 As of the greene leaues on a thicke tree, some fall, and some grow; so is the generation of flesh and blood, one commeth to an end, and another is borne.
text.NABRE: 14:18 As with the leaves growing on a luxuriant tree— one falls off and another sprouts— So with the generations of flesh and blood: one dies and another flourishes.
text.NRSV: Like abundant leaves on a spreading tree that sheds some and puts forth others, so are the generations of flesh and blood: one dies and another is born.

text.KJV: 14:20 Blessed is the man that doeth meditate good things in wisdome, and that reasoneth of holy things by his vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: 14:20 The Search for Wisdom and Her Blessings - Happy those who meditate on Wisdom, and fix their gaze on knowledge;
text.NRSV: Happy is the person who meditates on wisdom and reasons with intelligent understanding,

text.KJV: 14:22 Goe after her as one that traceth, and lie in wait in her wayes.
text.NABRE: 14:22 Who pursue her like a scout, and watch at her entry way;
text.NRSV: who pursues her like a hunter, and lies in wait on her paths;

text.KJV: 14:26 He shal set his children vnder her shelter, and shall lodge vnder her branches.
text.NABRE: 14:26 Who build their nest in her leaves, and lodge in her branches;
text.NRSV: he will place his children under her shelter, and will dwell under her branches;

text.KJV: 14:27 By her he shall be couered from heat, and in her glory shall he dwell.
text.NABRE: 14:27 Who take refuge from the heat in her shade and dwell in her home.
text.NRSV: he will be sheltered by her from the heat, and will dwell in the midst of her glory.

text.KJV: 15:1 He that feareth the Lord will doe good, and he that hath the knowledge of the Law shal obtaine her.
text.NABRE: 15:1 Chapter 15 - Whoever fears the LORD will do this; whoever is practiced in the Law will come to Wisdom.
text.NRSV: Whoever fears the Lord will do this, and whoever holds to the law will obtain wisdom.

text.KJV: 15:2 And as a mother shall she meet him, and receiue him as a wife maried of a virgin.
text.NABRE: 15:2 She will meet him like a mother; like a young bride she will receive him,
text.NRSV: She will come to meet him like a mother, and like a young bride she will welcome him.

text.KJV: 15:3 With the bread of vnderstanding shall she feed him, and giue him the water of wisedome to drinke.
text.NABRE: 15:3 She will feed him with the bread of learning, and give him the water of understanding to drink.
text.NRSV: She will feed him with the bread of learning, and give him the water of wisdom to drink.

text.KJV: 15:6 He shall finde ioy, and a crowne of gladnesse, and she shall cause him to inherit an euerlasting name.
text.NABRE: 15:6 Joy and gladness he will find, and an everlasting name he will inherit.
text.NRSV: He will find gladness and a crown of rejoicing, and will acquire an everlasting name.

text.KJV: 15:11 Say not thou, It is through the Lord, that I fell away, for thou oughtest not to doe the things that he hateth.
text.NABRE: 15:11 Free Will - Do not say: “It was God’s doing that I fell away,” for what he hates he does not do.
text.NRSV: Do not say, “It was the Lord’s doing that I fell away”; for he does not do what he hates.

text.KJV: 15:12 Say not thou, He hath caused mee to erre, for hee hath no need of the sinfull man.
text.NABRE: 15:12 Do not say: “He himself has led me astray,” for he has no need of the wicked.
text.NRSV: Do not say, “It was he who led me astray”; for he has no need of the sinful.

text.KJV: 15:13 The Lord hateth all abomination, and they that feare God loue it not.
text.NABRE: 15:13 Abominable wickedness the LORD hates and he does not let it happen to those who fear him.
text.NRSV: The Lord hates all abominations; such things are not loved by those who fear him.

text.KJV: 15:14 Hee himselfe made man from the beginning, and left him in the hand of his counsell,
text.NABRE: 15:14 God in the beginning created human beings and made them subject to their own free choice.
text.NRSV: It was he who created humankind in the beginning, and he left them in the power of their own free choice.

text.KJV: 15:15 If thou wilt, to keepe the Commandements, and to performe acceptable faithfulnesse.
text.NABRE: 15:15 If you choose, you can keep the commandments; loyalty is doing the will of God.
text.NRSV: If you choose, you can keep the commandments, and to act faithfully is a matter of your own choice.

text.KJV: 15:16 He hath set fire and water before thee: stretch forth thy hand vnto whether thou wilt.
text.NABRE: 15:16 Set before you are fire and water; to whatever you choose, stretch out your hand.
text.NRSV: He has placed before you fire and water; stretch out your hand for whichever you choose.

text.KJV: 15:17 Before man is life and death, and whether him liketh shalbe giuen him.
text.NABRE: 15:17 Before everyone are life and death, whichever they choose will be given them.
text.NRSV: Before each person are life and death, and whichever one chooses will be given.

text.KJV: 15:18 For the wisedome of the Lord is great, and he is mighty in power, and beholdeth all things,
text.NABRE: 15:18 Immense is the wisdom of the LORD; mighty in power, he sees all things.
text.NRSV: For great is the wisdom of the Lord; he is mighty in power and sees everything;

text.KJV: 15:19 And his eyes are vpon them that feare him, & hee knoweth euery worke of man.
text.NABRE: 15:19 The eyes of God behold his works, and he understands every human deed.
text.NRSV: his eyes are on those who fear him, and he knows every human action.

text.KJV: 15:20 Hee hath commanded no man to do wickedly, neither hath he giuen any man license to sinne.
text.NABRE: 15:20 He never commands anyone to sin, nor shows leniency toward deceivers.
text.NRSV: He has not commanded anyone to be wicked, and he has not given anyone permission to sin.

text.KJV: 16:1 Desire not a multitude of vnprofitable children, neither delight in vngodly sonnes.
text.NABRE: 16:1 Chapter 16 God’s Punishment of Sinners - Do not yearn for worthless children, or rejoice in wicked offspring.
text.NRSV: Do not desire a multitude of worthless children, and do not rejoice in ungodly offspring.

text.KJV: 16:2 Though they multiply, reioyce not in them, except the feare of the Lord be with them.
text.NABRE: 16:2 Even if they be many, do not rejoice in them if they do not have fear of the LORD.
text.NRSV: If they multiply, do not rejoice in them, unless the fear of the Lord is in them.

text.KJV: 16:6 In the congregation of the vngodly, shall a fire be kindled, and in a rebellious nation, wrath is set on fire.
text.NABRE: 16:6 Against a sinful band fire is kindled, upon a godless people wrath blazes.
text.NRSV: In a gathering of sinners a fire is kindled, and in a disobedient nation wrath blazes up.

text.KJV: 16:7 Hee was not pacified towards the olde giants, who fell away in the strength of their foolishnesse.
text.NABRE: 16:7 He did not forgive the princes of old who rebelled long ago in their might.
text.NRSV: He did not forgive the ancient giants who revolted in their might.

text.KJV: 16:11 And if there be one stiffe-necked among the people, it is marueile, if he escape vnpunished; for mercy and wrath are with him, hee is mighty to forgiue, and to powre out displeasure.
text.NABRE: 16:11 Had there been but one stiff-necked person, it would be a wonder had he gone unpunished. For mercy and anger alike are with him; he remits and forgives, but also pours out wrath.
text.NRSV: And if there is even one stubborn person, it is a wonder if he could go unpunished. For mercy and wrath are with the Lord; he is mighty to forgive, and he pours out wrath.

text.KJV: 16:12 As his mercy is great, so is his correction also: he iudgeth a man according to his workes.
text.NABRE: 16:12 Great as his mercy is his punishment; he judges people, each according to their deeds.
text.NRSV: As great as is his mercy, so also is his chastisement; he judges a person according to one’s deeds.

text.KJV: 16:17 Say not thou, I will hide my selfe from the Lord: shall any remember me from aboue? I shall not be remembred among so many people: for what is my soule among such an infinite number of creatures?
text.NRSV: Do not say, “I am hidden from the Lord, and who from on high has me in mind? Among so many people I am unknown, for what am I in a boundless creation?”

text.KJV: 16:18 Behold, the heauen, and the heauen of heauens, the deepe and the earth, and all that therein is, shall be mooued when he shall visit.
text.NRSV: Behold, heaven and the highest heaven, the abyss and the earth, tremble at his visitation!

text.KJV: 16:26 The works of the Lord are done in iudgement from the beginning: and from the time he made them, hee disposed the parts thereof.
text.NRSV: When the Lord created his works from the beginning, and, in making them, determined their boundaries,

text.KJV: 16:27 Hee garnished his workes for euer, and in his hand are the chiefe of them vnto all generations: they neither labour, nor are weary, nor cease from their workes.
text.NRSV: he arranged his works in an eternal order, and their dominion for all generations of them. They neither hunger nor grow weary, and they do not abandon their tasks.

text.KJV: 17:1 The Lord created man of the earth, and turned him into it againe.
text.NABRE: 17:1 Chapter 17 Creation of Human Beings - The Lord created human beings from the earth, and makes them return to earth again.
text.NRSV: The Lord created human beings out of earth, and makes them return to it again.

text.KJV: 17:3 He endued them with strength by themselues, and made them according to his image,
text.NABRE: 17:3 He endowed them with strength like his own, and made them in his image.
text.NRSV: He endowed them with strength like his own, and made them in his own image.

text.KJV: 17:7 Withall, hee filled them with the knowledge of vnderstanding, & shewed them good and euill.
text.NRSV: He filled them with knowledge and understanding, and showed them good and evil.

text.KJV: 17:11 Beside this he gaue them knowledge, and the law of life for an heritage.
text.NRSV: He bestowed upon them knowledge, and gave them the law of life as a heritage.

text.KJV: 17:12 He made an euerlasting couenant with them, and shewed them his iudgements.
text.NRSV: He established with them an eternal covenant, and revealed to them his decrees.

text.KJV: 17:15 Their wayes are euer before him, and shall not be hid from his eyes.
text.NRSV: Their ways are always before him; they will not be hidden from his eyes.

text.KJV: 17:19 Therefore all their workes are as the Sunne before him, and his eyes are continually vpon their wayes.
text.NRSV: All their works are as the sun before him, and his eyes are continually upon their ways.

text.KJV: 17:24 But vnto them that repent, he granted them returne, and comforted those that faile in patience.
text.NRSV: But to those who repent he grants a return, and he encourages those who are losing their hope.

text.KJV: 17:25 Returne vnto the Lord, and forsake thy sinnes, make thy prayer before his face, and offend lesse.
text.NRSV: Turn to the Lord and forsake your sins; pray in his presence and lessen your offense.

text.KJV: 17:26 Turne againe to the most High, and turne away from iniquitie: for he will leade thee out of darkenesse into the light of health, and hate thou abomination vehemently.
text.NRSV: Return to the Most High and turn away from iniquity, and hate intensely what he abhors.

text.KJV: 17:28 Thankesgiuing perisheth from the dead, as from one that is not: the liuing and sound in heart, shall praise the Lord.
text.NRSV: How great is the mercy of the Lord, and his forgiveness for those who return to him!

text.KJV: 18:1 Hee that liueth for euer, created all things in generall.
text.NABRE: 18:1 Chapter 18 The Divine Power and Mercy - He who lives forever created the whole universe;
text.NRSV: He who lives forever created the whole universe.

text.KJV: 18:8 What is man, and whereto serueth he? what is his good, & what is his euil?
text.NRSV: What are mortals, and of what use are they? What is their good and what is their evil?

text.KJV: 18:9 The number of a mans dayes at the most are an hundred yeeres.
text.NRSV: The number of their days is great if they reach a hundred years.

text.KJV: 18:10 As a drop of water vnto the Sea, and a grauell stone in comparison of the sand, so are a thousand yeeres to the dayes of eternitie.
text.NRSV: Like a drop of water from the sea and a grain of sand, so are a few years among the days of eternity.

text.KJV: 18:11 Therfore is God patient with them, & powreth forth his mercy vpon them.
text.NRSV: Therefore the Lord is patient with them and pours out his mercy upon them.

text.KJV: 18:13 The mercy of man is toward his neighbour, but the mercy of the Lord is vpon all flesh: he reprooueth and nurtureth, and teacheth, & bringeth againe as a shepheard his flocke.
text.NRSV: The compassion of human beings is for their neighbors, but the compassion of the Lord is for every living thing.

text.KJV: 18:15 My sonne, blemish not thy good deeds, neither vse vncomfortable words when thou giuest any thing.
text.NRSV: My child, do not mix reproach with your good deeds, or bitter words with any gift.

text.KJV: 18:19 Learne before thou speake, and vse phisicke, or euer thou be sicke.
text.NRSV: Before you speak, learn; and before you fall sick, take care of yourself.

text.KJV: 18:20 Before iudgement examine thy selfe, and in the day of visitation thou shalt find mercy.
text.NRSV: Before judgment comes, examine yourself; and at the time of scrutiny you will find forgiveness.

text.KJV: 18:30 Goe not after thy lustes, but refraine thy selfe from thine appetites.
text.NRSV: Do not follow your base desires, but restrain your appetites.

text.KJV: 19:1 A labouring man that is giuen to drunkennesse shal not be rich, and hee that contemneth small things shall fall by little & little.
text.NABRE: 19:1 Chapter 19 - Whoever does this grows no richer; those who waste the little they have will be stripped bare.
text.NRSV: A worker who is a drunkard will never become rich; one who despises small things will fail little by little.

text.KJV: 19:2 Wine and women will make men of vnderstanding to fall away, and he that cleaueth to harlots will become impudent.
text.NABRE: 19:2 Wine and women make the heart lustful, and the companion of prostitutes becomes reckless.
text.NRSV: Wine and women lead intelligent men astray, and the man who consorts with prostitutes is reckless.

text.KJV: 19:4 He that is hasty to giue credit is light minded, and he that sinneth shall offend against his owne soule.
text.NABRE: 19:4 Whoever trusts others too quickly has a shallow mind, and those who sin wrong themselves.
text.NRSV: One who trusts others too quickly is light-minded, and one who sins does wrong to himself.

text.KJV: 19:13 Admonish a friend, it may be he hath not done it, and if he haue that he doe it no more.
text.NABRE: 19:13 Admonish your friend—he may not have done it; and if he did, that he may not do it again.
text.NRSV: Question a friend; perhaps he did not do it; or if he did, so that he may not do it again.

text.KJV: 19:14 Admonish thy friend, it may be he hath not said it, and if he haue, that he speake it not againe.
text.NABRE: 19:14 Admonish your neighbor—he may not have said it; and if he did, that he may not say it again.
text.NRSV: Question a neighbor; perhaps he did not say it; or if he said it, so that he may not repeat it.

text.KJV: 19:15 Admonish a friend: for many times it is a slander, & beleeue not euery tale.
text.NABRE: 19:15 Admonish your friend—often it may be slander; do not believe every story.
text.NRSV: Question a friend, for often it is only slander; do not believe everything you hear.

text.KJV: 19:20 The feare of the Lord is all wisedome, and in all wisedome is the performance of the Law, and the knowledge of his omnipotencie.
text.NRSV: All wisdom is the fear of the Lord, and in all wisdom there is the fulfillment of the law.

text.KJV: 19:24 He that hath smal vnderstanding and feareth God, is better then one that hath much wisedome, and transgresseth the Law of the most High.
text.NRSV: Better are the God-fearing who lack understanding than the highly intelligent who transgress the law.

text.KJV: 20:1 There is a reproofe that is not comely: againe some man holdeth his tongue, and he is wise.
text.NABRE: 20:1 Chapter 20 Conduct of the Wise and the Foolish - There is an admonition that is untimely, but the silent person is the wise one.
text.NRSV: There is a rebuke that is untimely, and there is the person who is wise enough to keep silent.

text.KJV: 20:2 It is much better to reprooue, then to be angry secretly, and he that confesseth his fault, shall be preserued from hurt.
text.NABRE: 20:2 It is much better to admonish than to lose one’s temper;
text.NRSV: How much better it is to rebuke than to fume!

text.KJV: 20:5 There is one that keepeth silence and is found wise: and another by much babling becommeth hatefull.
text.NABRE: 20:5 One is silent and is thought wise; another, for being talkative, is disliked.
text.NRSV: One person keeps silent and is found to be wise; another is detested for being talkative.

text.KJV: 20:7 A wise man wil hold his tongue till he see opportunitie: but a babler and a foole will regard no time.
text.NABRE: 20:7 The wise remain silent till the right time comes, but a boasting fool misses the proper time.
text.NRSV: A wise person will keep silent until the right moment, but a boasting fool misses the right moment.

text.KJV: 20:18 To slip vpon a pauement, is better then to slip with the tongue: so, the fall of the wicked shall come speedily.
text.NABRE: 20:18 A slip on the floor is better than a slip of the tongue; in like manner the downfall of the wicked comes quickly.
text.NRSV: A slip on the pavement is better than a slip of the tongue; so the downfall of the wicked will come speedily.

text.KJV: 20:24 A lie is a foule blot in a man, yet it is continually in the mouth of the vntaught.
text.NABRE: 20:24 A lie is a foul blot in a person, yet it is always on the lips of the ignorant.
text.NRSV: A lie is a foul blot on a person; it is continually on the mouth of the ignorant.

text.KJV: 20:27 A wise man shall promote himselfe to honour with his words: and hee that hath vnderstanding, will please great men.
text.NABRE: 20:27 The wise gain promotion with few words, the prudent please the great.
text.NRSV: The wise person advances himself by his words, and one who is sensible pleases the great.
