text.KJV: 20:30 Wisedome that is hidde, and treasure that is hoarded vp, what profit is in them both?
text.NABRE: 20:30 Hidden wisdom and unseen treasure— what value has either?
text.NRSV: Hidden wisdom and unseen treasure, of what value is either?

text.KJV: 20:31 Better is he that hideth his folly, then a man that hideth his wisedome.
text.NABRE: 20:31 Better are those who hide their folly than those who hide their wisdom.
text.NRSV: Better is the person who hides his folly than the one who hides his wisdom.

text.KJV: 21:1 My sonne, hast thou sinned? doe so no more, but aske pardon for thy former sinnes.
text.NABRE: 21:1 Chapter 21 Dangers from Sin - My child, if you have sinned, do so no more, and for your past sins pray to be forgiven.
text.NRSV: Have you sinned, my child? Do so no more, but ask forgiveness for your past sins.

text.KJV: 21:2 Flee from sinne as from the face of a Serpent: for if thou commest too neere it, it will bite thee: the teeth thereof, are as the teeth of a lyon, slaying the soules of men.
text.NABRE: 21:2 Flee from sin as from a serpent that will bite you if you go near it; Its teeth, lion’s teeth, destroying human lives.
text.NRSV: Flee from sin as from a snake; for if you approach it, it will bite you; its teeth are lion’s teeth, and can destroy human lives.

text.KJV: 21:3 All iniquitie is as a two edged sword, the wounds whereof cannot be healed.
text.NABRE: 21:3 All lawlessness is like a two-edged sword; when it cuts, there is no healing.
text.NRSV: All lawlessness is like a two-edged sword; there is no healing for the wound it inflicts.

text.KJV: 21:5 A prayer out of a poore mans mouth reacheth to the eares of God, and his iudgement commeth speedily.
text.NABRE: 21:5 Prayer from the lips of the poor is heard at once, and justice is quickly granted them.
text.NRSV: The prayer of the poor goes from their lips to the ears of God, and his judgment comes speedily.

text.KJV: 21:10 The way of sinners is made plaine with stones, but at the ende thereof is the pit of hell.
text.NABRE: 21:10 The path of sinners is smooth stones, but its end is the pit of Sheol.
text.NRSV: The way of sinners is paved with smooth stones, but at its end is the pit of Hades.

text.KJV: 21:15 If a skilfull man heare a wise word, hee will commend it, and adde vnto it: but assoone as one of no vnderstanding heareth it, it displeaseth him, and he casteth it behinde his backe.
text.NABRE: 21:15 When the intelligent hear a wise saying, they praise it and add to it. The wanton hear it with distaste and cast it behind their back.
text.NRSV: When an intelligent person hears a wise saying, he praises it and adds to it;

text.KJV: 21:16 The talking of a foole is like a burden in the way: but grace shall be found in the lips of the wise.
text.NABRE: 21:16 A fool’s chatter is like a load on a journey, but delight is to be found on the lips of the intelligent.
text.NRSV: The chatter of a fool is like a burden on a journey, but delight is found in the speech of the intelligent.

text.KJV: 21:20 A foole lifteth vp his voyce with laughter, but a wise man doeth scarce smile a litle.
text.NABRE: 21:20 Fools raise their voice in laughter, but the prudent at most smile quietly.
text.NRSV: A fool raises his voice when he laughs, but a clever person smiles quietly.

text.KJV: 21:26 The heart of fooles is in their mouth, but the mouth of the wise is in their heart.
text.NABRE: 21:26 The mind of fools is in their mouths, but the mouth of the wise is in their mind.
text.NRSV: The heart of fools is in their mouths, but the mouth of the wise is in their heart.

text.KJV: 22:1 A slouthful man is compared to a filthy stone, and euery one will hisse him out to his disgrace.
text.NABRE: 22:1 Chapter 22 On Laziness and Foolishness - The sluggard is like a filthy stone; everyone hisses at his disgrace.
text.NRSV: The sluggard is like a filthy stone, and everyone hisses at his disgrace.

text.KJV: 22:3 An euill nurtured sonne is the dishonour of his father that begate him: and a daughter is borne to his losse.
text.NABRE: 22:3 An undisciplined child is a disgrace to its father; if it be a daughter, she brings him to poverty.
text.NRSV: It is a disgrace to be the father of an undisciplined son, and the birth of a daughter is a loss.

text.KJV: 22:4 A wise daughter shall bring an inheritance to her husband: but shee that liueth dishonestly, is her fathers heauinesse.
text.NABRE: 22:4 A thoughtful daughter obtains a husband of her own; a shameless one is her father’s grief.
text.NRSV: A sensible daughter obtains a husband of her own, but one who acts shamefully is a grief to her father.

text.KJV: 22:7 Who so teacheth a foole, is as one that gleweth a potsheard together, and as hee that waketh one from a sound sleepe.
text.NRSV: To teach a fool is like gluing a broken pot, or like waking a sleeper from a deep slumber.

text.KJV: 22:11 Weepe for the dead, for hee hath lost the light: and weepe for the foole, for he wanteth vnderstanding: make litle weeping for the dead, for hee is at rest: but the life of the foole is worse then death.
text.NRSV: Weep for the dead, for he has left the light behind; and weep for the fool, for he has left intelligence behind.

text.KJV: 22:12 Seuen dayes doe men mourne for him that is dead; but for a foole, and an vngodly man, all the dayes of his life.
text.NRSV: Mourning for the dead lasts seven days, but for a fool or an ungodly person it lasts all the days of their lives.

text.KJV: 22:22 If thou hast opened thy mouth against thy friend, feare not, for there may be a reconciliation: except for vpbraiding, or pride, or disclosing of secrets, or a treacherous wound, for, for these things euery friend will depart.
text.NRSV: If you have opened your mouth against a friend, do not worry, for reconciliation is possible. But as for reviling, arrogance, disclosure of secrets, or a treacherous blow—in these cases any friend will flee.

text.KJV: 22:27 Who shall set a watch before my mouth, and a seale of wisedome vpon my lippes, that I fall not suddenly by them, & that my tongue destroy me not?
text.NRSV: Who will set a guard over my mouth, and an effective seal upon my lips, so that I may not fall because of them, and my tongue may not destroy me?

text.KJV: 23:1 O Lord, father and gouernour of all my whole life, leaue me not to their counsels, and let me not fall by them.
text.NABRE: 23:1 Chapter 23 - Lord, Father and Master of my life, do not abandon me to their designs, do not let me fall because of them!
text.NRSV: O Lord, Father and Master of my life, do not abandon me to their designs, and do not let me fall because of them!

text.KJV: 23:7 Heare, O yee children, the discipline of the mouth: He that keepeth it, shall neuer be taken in his lippes.
text.NABRE: 23:7 Proper Use of the Tongue - Listen, my children, to instruction concerning the mouth, for whoever keeps it will not be ensnared.
text.NRSV: Listen, my children, to instruction about the mouth; the one who keeps it will not be caught.

text.KJV: 23:9 Accustome not thy mouth to swearing: neither vse thy selfe to the naming of the holy one.
text.NABRE: 23:9 Do not accustom your mouth to oaths, or habitually utter the Holy Name.
text.NRSV: Do not accustom your mouth to oaths, nor habitually utter the name of the Holy One.

text.KJV: 23:18 A man that breaketh wedlocke, saying thus in his heart, Who seeth me? I am compassed about with darknesse: the walles couer me; & no body seeth me, what neede I to feare? The most high wil not remember my sinnes:
text.NABRE: 23:18 The man who dishonors his marriage bed says to himself, “Who can see me? Darkness surrounds me, walls hide me, no one sees me. Who can stop me from sinning?” He is not mindful of the Most High,
text.NRSV: A man who violates his marriage bed says to himself, “Who can see me? Darkness surrounds me, the walls hide me, and no one sees me; why should I fear? The Most High will not remember my sins.”

text.KJV: 23:19 Such a man only feareth the eies of men, and knoweth not that the eies of the Lord are tenne thousand times brighter then the Sunne, beholding all the waies of men, and considering the most secret parts.
text.NABRE: 23:19 fearing only human eyes. He does not realize that the eyes of the Lord, ten thousand times brighter than the sun, Observe every step taken and peer into hidden corners.
text.NRSV: His fear is confined to human eyes, and he does not realize that the eyes of the Lord are ten thousand times brighter than the sun.

text.KJV: 23:22 Thus shall it goe also with the wife, that leaueth her husband, and bringeth in an heire by another:
text.NABRE: 23:22 So it is with the woman unfaithful to her husband, who offers him an heir by another man.
text.NRSV: So it is with a woman who leaves her husband and presents him with an heir by another man.

text.KJV: 23:27 And they that remaine, shall know that there is nothing better then the feare of the Lord, and that there is nothing sweeter then to take heed vnto the Commandement of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 23:27 Thus all who dwell on the earth shall know, all who remain in the world shall understand, That nothing is better than the fear of the Lord, nothing sweeter than obeying the commandments of the Lord.
text.NRSV: Those who survive will know that nothing is better than the fear of the Lord, and nothing sweeter than to heed the Lord’s commandments.

text.KJV: 24:1 Wisedome shall praise her selfe, and shall glory in the midst of her people.
text.NABRE: 24:1 Chapter 24 Praise of Wisdom - Wisdom sings her own praises, among her own people she proclaims her glory.
text.NRSV: Wisdom praises herself, and tells of her glory in the midst of her people.

text.KJV: 24:2 In the Congregation of the most high, shall she open her mouth, and triumph before his power.
text.NABRE: 24:2 In the assembly of the Most High she opens her mouth, in the presence of his host she tells of her glory:
text.NRSV: In the assembly of the Most High she opens her mouth, and in the presence of his hosts she tells of her glory:

text.KJV: 24:3 I came out of the mouth of the most High, and couered the earth as a cloud.
text.NABRE: 24:3 “From the mouth of the Most High I came forth, and covered the earth like a mist.
text.NRSV: “I came forth from the mouth of the Most High, and covered the earth like a mist.”

text.KJV: 24:8 So the creatour of all things gaue mee a commandement, and hee that made me, caused my tabernacle to rest: and said, Let thy dwelling be in Iacob, and thine inheritance in Israel.
text.NABRE: 24:8 “Then the Creator of all gave me his command, and my Creator chose the spot for my tent. He said, ‘In Jacob make your dwelling, in Israel your inheritance.’
text.NRSV: “Then the Creator of all things gave me a command, and my Creator chose the place for my tent. He said, ‘Make your dwelling in Jacob, and in Israel receive your inheritance.’”

text.KJV: 24:9 Hee created mee from the beginning before the world, and I shall neuer faile.
text.NABRE: 24:9 Before all ages, from the beginning, he created me, and through all ages I shall not cease to be.
text.NRSV: “Before the ages, in the beginning, he created me, and for all the ages I shall not cease to be.”

text.KJV: 24:19 Come vnto me all ye that be desirous of mee, and fill your selues with my fruits.
text.NRSV: “Come to me, you who desire me, and eat your fill of my fruits.”

text.KJV: 24:20 For my memorial is sweeter then hony, and mine inheritance then the hony combe.
text.NRSV: “For the memory of me is sweeter than honey, and the possession of me sweeter than the honeycomb.”

text.KJV: 24:21 They that eate mee shall yet be hungry, and they that drinke me shall yet be thirstie.
text.NRSV: “Those who eat of me will hunger for more, and those who drink of me will thirst for more.”

text.KJV: 24:23 All these things are the booke of the Couenant of the most high God, euen the Law which Moses commanded for an heritage vnto the Congregations of Iacob.
text.NRSV: All this is the book of the covenant of the Most High God, the law that Moses commanded us as an inheritance for the congregations of Jacob.

text.KJV: 25:1 In three things I was beautified, and stoode vp beautiful, both before God and men: the vnitie of brethren, the loue of neighbours, a man and a wife that agree together.
text.NABRE: 25:1 Chapter 25 Those Who Are Worthy of Praise - With three things I am delighted, for they are pleasing to the Lord and to human beings: Harmony among relatives, friendship among neighbors, and a wife and a husband living happily together.
text.NRSV: I take pleasure in three things, and they are beautiful in the sight of God and of mortals: agreement among brothers and sisters, friendship among neighbors, and a wife and a husband who live in harmony.

text.KJV: 25:2 Three sorts of men my soule hateth, and I am greatly offended at their life: a poore man that is proud, a rich man that is a lyar, and an olde adulterer that doteth.
text.NABRE: 25:2 Three kinds of people I hate, and I loathe their manner of life: A proud pauper, a rich liar, and a lecherous old fool.
text.NRSV: I detest three kinds of people... a beggar who is proud, a rich man who is a liar, and an adulterous old man who lacks good sense.

text.KJV: 25:7 There be nine things which I haue iudged in mine heart to be happy, and the tenth I will vtter with my tongue: a man that hath ioy of his children, and he that liueth to see the fall of his enemie.
text.NABRE: 25:7 There are nine who come to mind as blessed, a tenth whom my tongue proclaims: The man who finds joy in his children, and the one who lives to see the downfall of his enemies.
text.NRSV: I can think of nine whom I would call blessed, and a tenth my tongue proclaims: a man who can rejoice in his children; a man who lives to see the downfall of his enemies.

text.KJV: 25:8 Well is him that dwelleth with a wife of vnderstanding, and that hath not slipped with his tongue, and that hath not serued a man more vnworthy then himselfe.
text.NABRE: 25:8 Happy the man who lives with a sensible woman, and the one who does not plow with an ox and a donkey combined. Happy the one who does not sin with the tongue, who does not serve an inferior.
text.NRSV: Happy is the one who lives with a sensible wife, and the one who does not plow with ox and ass together. Happy is the one who does not slip with the tongue, and the one who has not served a man inferior to himself.

text.KJV: 25:9 Well is him that hath found prudence, and he that speaketh in the eares of him that will heare.
text.NABRE: 25:9 Happy the one who finds a friend, who speaks to attentive ears.
text.NRSV: Happy is the one who finds a friend, and the one who speaks to attentive listeners.

text.KJV: 25:10 Oh how great is he that findeth wisedome! yet is there none aboue him that feareth the Lord.
text.NABRE: 25:10 How great is the one who finds wisdom, but none is greater than the one who fears the Lord.
text.NRSV: How great is the one who finds wisdom! But none is superior to the one who fears the Lord.

text.KJV: 25:24 Of the woman came the beginning of sinne, & through her wee all die.
text.NRSV: From a woman sin had its beginning, and because of her we all die.

text.KJV: 26:1 Blessed is the man that hath a vertuous wife, for the number of his dayes shall be double.
text.NABRE: 26:1 Chapter 26 - Happy the husband of a good wife; the number of his days will be doubled.
text.NRSV: Happy is the husband of a good wife; the number of his days will be doubled.

text.KJV: 26:2 A vertuous woman reioyceth her husband, and he shall fulfill the yeeres of his life in peace.
text.NABRE: 26:2 A loyal wife brings joy to her husband, and he will finish his years in peace.
text.NRSV: A loyal wife brings joy to her husband, and he will finish his years in peace.

text.KJV: 26:3 A good wife is a good portion, which shall be giuen in the portion of them that feare the Lord.
text.NABRE: 26:3 A good wife is a generous gift bestowed upon him who fears the Lord.
text.NRSV: A good wife is a great blessing; she will be granted among the blessings of the man who fears the Lord.

text.KJV: 26:13 The grace of a wife delighteth her husband, and her discretion will fat his bones.
text.NABRE: 26:13 A gracious wife delights her husband; her thoughtfulness puts flesh on his bones.
text.NRSV: A wife’s charm delights her husband, and her skill puts fat on his bones.

text.KJV: 26:14 A silent and louing woman is a gift of the Lord, and there is nothing so much worth, as a mind well instructed.
text.NABRE: 26:14 A silent wife is a gift from the Lord; nothing is worth more than her self-discipline.
text.NRSV: A silent wife is a gift from the Lord, and nothing is so precious as her self-discipline.

text.KJV: 26:28 There be two things that grieue my heart: and the third maketh me angry: a man of warre that suffereth pouerty, and men of vnderstanding that are not set by: and one that returneth from righteousnesse to sinne: the Lord prepareth such a one for the sword.
text.NRSV: Two things grieve my heart, and a third makes me angry: a warrior in want through poverty, intelligent men who are treated with contempt, and a man who turns back from righteousness to sin—the Lord will prepare him for the sword!

text.KJV: 26:29 A merchant shall hardly keepe himselfe from doing wrong: and an huckster shall not be freed from sinne.
text.NRSV: A merchant can hardly keep from wrongdoing, nor is a tradesman innocent of sin.

text.KJV: 27:1 Many haue sinned for a smal matter: & he that seeketh for abundance will turne his eies away.
text.NABRE: 27:1 Chapter 27 - For the sake of profit many sin, and the struggle for wealth blinds the eyes.
text.NRSV: Many have sinned for the sake of profit, and one who seeks to grow rich must turn away his eyes.

text.KJV: 27:2 As a naile sticketh fast betweene the ioynings of the stones: so doth sinne sticke close betweene buying and selling.
text.NABRE: 27:2 A stake will be driven between fitted stones— sin will be wedged in between buying and selling.
text.NRSV: As a stake is driven firmly into a fissure between stones, so sin is wedged in between selling and buying.

text.KJV: 27:3 Unlesse a man hold himselfe diligently in the feare of the Lord, his house shall soone be ouerthrowen.
text.NABRE: 27:3 Unless one holds fast to the fear of the Lord, with sudden swiftness will one’s house be thrown down.
text.NRSV: Unless a person holds zealously to the fear of the Lord, his house will be quickly overthrown.

text.KJV: 27:4 As when one sifteth with a sieue, the refuse remaineth, so the filth of man in his talke.
text.NABRE: 27:4 When a sieve is shaken, the husks appear; so do people’s faults when they speak.
text.NRSV: When a sieve is shaken, the refuse appears; so do a person’s faults when he speaks.

text.KJV: 27:5 The furnace prooueth the potters vessell: so the triall of man is in his reasoning.
text.NABRE: 27:5 The furnace tests the potter’s vessels; the test of a person is in conversation.
text.NRSV: The kiln tests the potter’s vessels; so the test of a person is in his conversation.

text.KJV: 27:6 The fruite declareth if the tree haue beene dressed: so is the vtterance of a conceit in the heart of man.
text.NABRE: 27:6 The fruit of a tree shows the care it has had; so speech discloses the bent of a person’s heart.
text.NRSV: Its fruit discloses the cultivation of a tree; so a person’s speech discloses the cultivation of his mind.

text.KJV: 27:16 Who so discouereth secrets, looseth his credit: and shall neuer find friend to his minde.
text.NABRE: 27:16 Whoever betrays a secret destroys confidence, and will never find a congenial friend.
text.NRSV: One who discloses secrets destroys confidence, and will never find a congenial friend.

text.KJV: 27:17 Loue thy friend, and be faithfull vnto him: but if thou bewrayest his secrets, follow no more after him.
text.NABRE: 27:17 Cherish your friend, keep faith with him; but if you betray his secrets, do not go after him;
text.NRSV: Love your friend and keep faith with him; but if you disclose his secrets, do not follow after him.

text.KJV: 27:22 He that winketh with the eies worketh euil, and he that knoweth him will depart from him.
text.NABRE: 27:22 Malice, Anger and Vengeance - Whoever has shifty eyes plots mischief and those who know him will keep their distance;
text.NRSV: Whoever winks with the eye plots mischief, and those who know him will keep their distance.

text.KJV: 28:1 He that reuengeth shall find vengeance from the Lord, and he will surely keepe his sinnes (in remembrance.)
text.NABRE: 28:1 Chapter 28 - The vengeful will face the Lord’s vengeance; indeed he remembers their sins in detail.
text.NRSV: The vengeful will face the Lord’s vengeance, for he keeps a strict account of their sins.

text.KJV: 28:2 Forgiue thy neighbour the hurt that he hath done vnto thee, so shall thy sinnes also be forgiuen when thou prayest.
text.NABRE: 28:2 Forgive your neighbor the wrong done to you; then when you pray, your own sins will be forgiven.
text.NRSV: Forgive your neighbor the wrong he has done, and then your sins will be pardoned when you pray.

text.KJV: 28:3 One man beareth hatred against another, and doeth he seeke pardon from the Lord?
text.NABRE: 28:3 Does anyone nourish anger against another and expect healing from the LORD?
text.NRSV: Does anyone harbor anger against another, and expect healing from the Lord?

text.KJV: 28:4 Hee sheweth no mercy to a man, which is like himselfe: and doeth hee aske forgiuenesse of his owne sinnes?
text.NABRE: 28:4 Can one refuse mercy to a sinner like oneself, yet seek pardon for one’s own sins?
text.NRSV: If one has no mercy toward another like himself, can he then beg for pardon of his own sins?

text.KJV: 28:6 Remember thy end, and let enimitie cease, corruption and death, and abide in the Commandements.
text.NABRE: 28:6 Remember your last days and set enmity aside; remember death and decay, and cease from sin!
text.NRSV: Remember the end of your life, and set enmity aside; remember corruption and death, and be true to the commandments.

text.KJV: 28:12 If thou blow the sparke, it shall burne: if thou spit vpon it, it shall bee quenched, and both these come out of thy mouth.
text.NABRE: 28:12 The Evil Tongue - If you blow on a spark, it turns into flame, if you spit on it, it dies out; yet both you do with your mouth!
text.NRSV: If you blow on a spark, it will glow; if you spit on it, it will be put out; yet both come out of your mouth.

text.KJV: 28:13 Curse the whisperer, and double tongued: for such haue destroyed many that were at peace.
text.NABRE: 28:13 Cursed be gossips and the double-tongued, for they destroy the peace of many.
text.NRSV: Curse the whisperer and the double-tongued, for they have destroyed many who were at peace.

text.KJV: 28:18 Many haue fallen by the edge of the sword: but not so many as haue fallen by the tongue.
text.NABRE: 28:18 Many have fallen by the edge of the sword, but not as many as by the tongue.
text.NRSV: Many have fallen by the edge of the sword, but not as many as have fallen because of the tongue.

text.KJV: 29:1 Hee that is mercifull, will lende vnto his neighbour, and hee that strengthneth his hande, keepeth the Commandements.
text.NABRE: 29:1 Chapter 29 Loans, Alms and Surety - The merciful lend to their neighbor, by holding out a helping hand, they keep the commandments.
text.NRSV: The merciful lend to their neighbors; by holding out a helping hand they keep the commandments.

text.KJV: 29:2 Lend to thy neighbour in time of his need, and pay thou thy neighbour againe in due season.
text.NABRE: 29:2 Lend to your neighbor in his time of need, and pay back your neighbor in time.
text.NRSV: Lend to your neighbor in his time of need; pay your neighbor back at the appointed time.

text.KJV: 29:8 Yet haue thou patience with a man in poore estate, and delay not to shew him mercy.
text.NABRE: 29:8 But with those in humble circumstances be patient; do not keep them waiting for your alms.
text.NRSV: Be patient with someone in humble circumstances, and do not keep him waiting for your alms.

text.KJV: 29:10 Lose thy money for thy brother and thy friend, and let it not rust vnder a stone to be lost.
text.NABRE: 29:10 Lose your money for relative or friend; do not hide it under a stone to rot.
text.NRSV: Lose your silver for the sake of a brother or a friend, and do not let it rust under a stone and be lost.

text.KJV: 29:11 Lay vp thy treasure according to the commandements of the most high, and it shall bring thee more profite then golde.
text.NABRE: 29:11 Dispose of your treasure according to the commandments of the Most High, and that will profit you more than the gold.
text.NRSV: Lay up your treasure according to the commandments of the Most High, and it will profit you more than gold.

text.KJV: 29:21 The chiefe thing for life is water and bread, and clothing, and an house to couer shame.
text.NABRE: 29:21 Frugality and Its Rewards - Life’s prime needs are water, bread, and clothing, and also a house for decent privacy.
text.NRSV: The essentials of life are water and bread and clothing, and a house to provide privacy.

text.KJV: 29:22 Better is the life of a poore man in a meane cottage, then delicate fare in another mans house.
text.NABRE: 29:22 Better is the life of the poor under the shadow of their own roof than sumptuous banquets among strangers.
text.NRSV: Better is the life of a poor person under the shelter of his own roof than sumptuous food in another’s house.

text.KJV: 29:28 These things are grieuous to a man of vnderstanding: the vpbraiding of house-roome, and reproching of the lender.
text.NABRE: 29:28 Painful things to a sensitive person are rebuke as a parasite and insults from creditors.
text.NRSV: To a sensible person these things are intolerable: expression of grievance from a household, and the insults of a moneylender.

text.KJV: 30:1 Hee that loueth his sonne, causeth him oft to feele the rodde, that hee may haue ioy of him in the end.
text.NABRE: 30:1 Chapter 30 The Training of Children - Whoever loves a son will chastise him often, that he may be his joy when he grows up.
text.NRSV: He who loves his son will whip him often, so that he may rejoice at the way he turns out.

text.KJV: 30:2 He that chastiseth his sonne, shall haue ioy in him, and shall reioyce of him among his acquaintance.
text.NABRE: 30:2 Whoever disciplines a son will benefit from him, and boast of him among acquaintances.
text.NRSV: He who disciplines his son will profit by him, and will boast of him among acquaintances.

text.KJV: 30:8 An horse not broken becommeth headstrong: and a childe left to himselfe will be wilfull.
text.NABRE: 30:8 An untamed horse turns out stubborn; and a son left to himself grows up unruly.
text.NRSV: A horse untamed grows stubborn, and a son left to himself grows headstrong.

text.KJV: 30:13 Chastise thy sonne, and hold him to labour, lest his lewd behauiour be an offence vnto thee.
text.NABRE: 30:13 Discipline your son and make heavy his yoke, lest you be offended by his shamelessness.
text.NRSV: Discipline your son and make his yoke heavy, so that you may not be offended by his shamelessness.

text.KJV: 30:14 Better is the poore being sound and strong of constitution, then a rich man that is afflicted in his body.
text.NABRE: 30:14 Health and Cheerfulness - Better the poor in vigorous health than the rich with bodily ills.
text.NRSV: Better off is a poor man who is well and strong in constitution than a rich man who is severely afflicted in body.

text.KJV: 30:15 Health and good state of body are aboue all gold, and a strong body aboue infinite wealth.
text.NABRE: 30:15 I would rather have bodily health than any gold, and contentment of spirit than pearls.
text.NRSV: Health and vigor are better than any gold, and a robust body than countless riches.

text.KJV: 30:17 Death is better then a bitter life, or continuall sickenesse.
text.NABRE: 30:17 Better is death than a wretched life, everlasting sleep than constant illness.
text.NRSV: Death is better than a miserable life, and eternal rest than chronic sickness.

text.KJV: 30:21 Giue not ouer thy mind to heauinesse, and afflict not thy selfe in thine owne counsell.
text.NABRE: 30:21 Do not give in to sadness, or torment yourself deliberately.
text.NRSV: Do not give yourself over to sorrow, and do not distress yourself deliberately.

text.KJV: 30:23 Loue thine owne soule, and comfort thy heart, remoue sorrow far from thee: for sorrow hath killed many, and there is no profit therein.
text.NABRE: 30:23 Distract yourself and renew your courage, drive resentment far away from you; For grief has killed many, and nothing is to be gained from resentment.
text.NRSV: Beguile yourself and comfort your heart, and drive sorrow far from you, for sorrow has destroyed many, and there is no profit in it.

text.KJV: 30:24 Enuie and wrath shorten the life, and carefulnesse bringeth age before the time.
text.NABRE: 30:24 Envy and anger shorten one’s days, and anxiety brings on premature old age.
text.NRSV: Jealousy and anger shorten life, and anxiety brings on premature old age.

text.KJV: 31:1 Watching for riches, consumeth the flesh, and the care therof driueth away sleepe.
text.NABRE: 31:1 Chapter 31 The Proper Attitude Toward Riches - Wakefulness over wealth wastes away the flesh, and anxiety over it drives away sleep.
text.NRSV: Wakefulness over wealth wastes the flesh, and anxiety about it drives away sleep.

text.KJV: 31:12 If thou sit at a bountifull table, bee not greedy vpon it, and say not, There is much meate on it.
text.NABRE: 31:12 Table Etiquette - Are you seated at the table of the great? Bring to it no greedy gullet, Nor say, “How much food there is here!”
text.NRSV: Are you seated at a great table? Do not be greedy at it, and do not say, “How much food there is here!”

text.KJV: 31:13 Remember that a wicked eye is an euill thing: and what is created more wicked then an eye? therefore it weepeth vpon euery occasion.
text.NABRE: 31:13 Remember that the greedy eye is evil. What has been created more greedy than the eye? Therefore, it weeps for any cause.
text.NRSV: Remember that a greedy eye is a bad thing; what has been created more greedy than an eye? Therefore it sheds tears from every face.

text.KJV: 31:16 Eate as it becommeth a man those things which are set before thee: and deuoure not, lest thou be hated.
text.NABRE: 31:16 Eat, like anyone else, what is set before you, but do not eat greedily, lest you be despised.
text.NRSV: Eat like a human being what is set before you, and do not chew greedily, or you will be despised.

text.KJV: 31:17 Leaue off first for maners sake, and be not vnsatiable, lest thou offend.
text.NABRE: 31:17 Be the first to stop, as befits good manners; and do not gorge yourself, lest you give offense.
text.NRSV: Be the first to stop, as befits good manners, and do not be insatiable, or you will give offense.

text.KJV: 31:25 Shew not thy valiantnesse in wine, for wine hath destroyed many.
text.NABRE: 31:25 Let not wine be the proof of your strength, for wine has been the ruin of many.
text.NRSV: Do not try to prove your strength by wine-drinking, for wine has destroyed many.

text.KJV: 31:27 Wine is as good as life to a man if it be drunke moderatly: what life is then to a man that is without wine? for it was made to make men glad.
text.NABRE: 31:27 Wine is very life to anyone, if taken in moderation. Does anyone really live who lacks the wine which from the beginning was created for joy?
text.NRSV: Wine is very life to human beings if drunk in moderation. What is life to one who is without wine? It has been created to make people happy.

text.KJV: 31:28 Wine measurably drunke, and in season, bringeth gladnesse of the heart and cheerefulnesse of the minde.
text.NABRE: 31:28 Joy of heart, good cheer, and delight is wine enough, drunk at the proper time.
text.NRSV: Wine drunk at the proper time and in moderation is rejoicing of heart and gladness of soul.

text.KJV: 31:30 Drunkennesse increaseth the rage of a foole till he offend, it diminisheth strength, and maketh wounds.
text.NABRE: 31:30 Wine in excess is a snare for the fool; it lessens strength and multiplies wounds.
text.NRSV: Drunkenness increases the anger of a fool to his own injury, reducing his strength and adding wounds.

text.KJV: 32:1 If thou be made the master (of the feast) lift not thy selfe vp, but bee among them as one of the rest, take diligent care for them, and so sit downe.
text.NABRE: 32:1 Chapter 32 - If you are chosen to preside at a dinner, do not be puffed up, but with the guests be as one of them; Take care of them first and then sit down;
text.NRSV: If they make you master of the feast, do not promote yourself; be among them as one of them.

text.KJV: 32:2 And when thou hast done all thy office, take thy place that thou mayest be merry with them, and receiue a crowne for thy well ordering of the feast.
text.NABRE: 32:2 see to their needs, and then take your place, To share in their joy and receive a wreath for a job well done.
text.NRSV: When you have performed all your duties, take your place, so that you may be merry along with them and receive a wreath for your efficiency.

text.KJV: 32:3 Speake thou that art the elder, for it becometh thee, but with sound iudgement, and hinder not musicke.
text.NABRE: 32:3 You who are older, it is your right to speak, but temper your knowledge and do not interrupt the singing.
text.NRSV: Speak, you who are older, for it is your right, but with accurate knowledge, and do not interrupt the music.

text.KJV: 32:7 Speake yong man, if there be need of thee: and yet scarsely when thou art twise asked:
text.NABRE: 32:7 Speak, young man, only when necessary, when they have asked you more than once.
text.NRSV: Speak, young man, if you are indispensable, but no more than twice, and only if asked.

text.KJV: 32:14 Who so feareth the Lord, will receiue his discipline, and they that seeke him early, shall find fauour.
text.NABRE: 32:14 The Providence of God - Whoever seeks God must accept discipline; and whoever resorts to him obtains an answer.
text.NRSV: The one who seeks God will accept his discipline, and those who rise early to seek him will find favor.

text.KJV: 32:23 In euery good worke trust thy owne soule: for this is the keeping of the commandements.
text.NABRE: 32:23 Whatever you do, be on your guard, for whoever does so keeps the commandments.
text.NRSV: In all that you do trust your own soul, for this is the keeping of the commandments.

text.KJV: 33:1 There shall no euill happen vnto him that feareth the Lord, but in temptation euen againe he will deliuer him.
text.NABRE: 33:1 Chapter 33 - No evil can harm the one who fears the LORD; through trials, again and again he is rescued.
text.NRSV: No evil will befall the one who fears the Lord, but in trials such a one will be delivered again and again.

text.KJV: 33:2 A wise man hateth not the Law, but he that is an hypocrite therein, is as a ship in a storme.
text.NABRE: 33:2 Whoever hates the law is without wisdom, and is tossed about like a boat in a storm.
text.NRSV: The wise person will not hate the law, but the one who is hypocritical about it is like a boat in a storm.

text.KJV: 33:7 Why doth one day excell another? when as all the light of euery day in the yeere is of the Sunne.
text.NABRE: 33:7 Why is one day more important than another, when the same sun lights up every day of the year?
text.NRSV: Why is one day more important than another, when all the daylight in the year is from the sun?

text.KJV: 33:8 By the knowledge of the Lord they were distinguished: and he altered seasons and feasts.
text.NABRE: 33:8 By the LORD’s knowledge they are kept distinct; and he designates the seasons and feasts.
text.NRSV: By the Lord’s knowledge they were kept distinct, and he appointed the different seasons and festivals.

text.KJV: 33:10 And all men are from the ground, and Adam was created of earth.
text.NABRE: 33:10 Likewise, all people are of clay, and from earth humankind was formed;
text.NRSV: All human beings come from the ground, and humankind was created out of the dust.

text.KJV: 33:11 In much knowledge the Lord hath diuided them, and made their wayes diuers.
text.NABRE: 33:11 In the fullness of his knowledge the Lord distinguished them, and he designated their different ways.
text.NRSV: In the fullness of his knowledge the Lord distinguished them and made their ways diverse.

text.KJV: 33:13 As the clay is in the potters hand to fashion it at his pleasure: so man is in the hand of him that made him, to render to them as liketh him best.
text.NABRE: 33:13 Like clay in the hands of a potter, to be molded according to his pleasure, So are people in the hands of their Maker, to be dealt with as he decides.
text.NRSV: Like clay in the hand of the potter, to be molded just as he pleases, so all are in the hand of their Maker, to be given whatever he decides.

text.KJV: 33:24 Fodder, a wand, and burdens, are for the asse: and bread, correction, and worke for a seruant.
text.NABRE: 33:24 When your few days reach their limit, at the time of death distribute your inheritance.
text.NRSV: Fodder and a stick and burdens for a donkey; bread and discipline and work for a slave.

text.KJV: 33:27 Sende him to labour that hee be not idle: for idlenesse teacheth much euill.
text.NABRE: 33:27 The yoke and harness will bow the neck; and for a wicked slave, punishment in the stocks.
text.NRSV: Put him to work, so that he may not be idle, for idleness teaches much evil.

text.KJV: 34:1 The hopes of a man voyd of vnderstanding are vaine, and false: and dreames lift vp fooles.
text.NABRE: 34:1 Chapter 34 Trust in the Lord and Not in Dreams - Empty and false are the hopes of the senseless, and dreams give wings to fools.
text.NRSV: The senseless have vain and false hopes, and dreams give wings to fools.

text.KJV: 34:2 Who so regardeth dreames, is like him that catcheth at a shadow, and followeth after the winde.
text.NABRE: 34:2 Like one grasping at shadows or chasing the wind, so anyone who believes in dreams.
text.NRSV: As one who catches at a shadow and pursues the wind, so is anyone who believes in dreams.

text.KJV: 34:14 Who so feareth the Lord, shall not feare nor be afraid, for hee is his hope.
text.NABRE: 34:14 Living is the spirit of those who fear the Lord,
text.NRSV: The one who fears the Lord will not be timid, or play the coward, for the Lord is his hope.

text.KJV: 34:15 Blessed is the soule of him that feareth the Lord: to whom doeth hee looke? and who is his strength?
text.NABRE: 34:15 for their hope is in their savior.
text.NRSV: Happy is the soul of the one who fears the Lord! To whom does he look? And who is his support?

text.KJV: 34:18 Hee that sacrificeth of a thing wrongfully gotten, his offering is ridiculous, and the giftes of vniust men are not accepted.
text.NABRE: 34:18 In whom does he trust, and who is his support?
text.NRSV: If one sacrifices from what has been wrongfully acquired, the offering is blemished; the gifts of the lawless are not acceptable.

text.KJV: 34:19 The most high is not pleased with the offerings of the wicked, neither is he pacified for sinne by the multitude of sacrifices.
text.NABRE: 34:19 The eyes of the Lord are upon those who love him; he is their mighty shield and strong support, A shelter from the heat, a shade from the noonday sun, a guard against stumbling, a help against falling.
text.NRSV: The Most High is not pleased with the offerings of the ungodly, nor for a multitude of sacrifices does he forgive sins.

text.KJV: 34:25 He that washeth himselfe after the touching of a dead body, if he touch it againe, what auaileth his washing?
text.NABRE: 34:25 The bread of charity is life itself for the needy; whoever withholds it is a murderer.
text.NRSV: If one washes after touching a corpse, and touches it again, what has been gained by washing?

text.KJV: 34:26 So is it with a man that fasteth for his sinnes, and goeth againe and doeth the same: who will heare his prayer, or what doeth his humbling profit him?
text.NABRE: 34:26 To take away a neighbor’s living is to commit murder;
text.NRSV: So if a person fasts for his sins, and goes again and does the same things, who will listen to his prayer? And what has he gained by humbling himself?

text.KJV: 35:1 Hee that keepeth the law, bringeth offerings enow: he that taketh heed to the commandement, offereth a peace offering.
text.NABRE: 35:1 Chapter 35 - To keep the law is to make many offerings;
text.NRSV: The one who keeps the law makes many offerings; one who heeds the commandments sacrifices a peace offering.

text.KJV: 35:2 He that requiteth a good turne, offereth fine floure: and he that giueth almes, sacrificeth praise.
text.NABRE: 35:2 whoever observes the commandments sacrifices a peace offering.
text.NRSV: The one who returns a kindness offers choice flour, and one who gives alms sacrifices a thank offering.

text.KJV: 35:3 To depart from wickednesse is a thing pleasing to the Lord: and to forsake vnrighteousnesse, is a propitiation.
text.NABRE: 35:3 By works of charity one offers fine flour,
text.NRSV: To keep from wickedness is pleasing to the Lord, and to forsake unrighteousness is an atonement.

text.KJV: 35:4 Thou shalt not appeare emptie before the Lord:
text.NABRE: 35:4 and one who gives alms presents a sacrifice of praise.
text.NRSV: Do not appear before the Lord empty-handed,

text.KJV: 35:5 For all these things because of the commandement.
text.NABRE: 35:5 To refrain from evil pleases the Lord, and to avoid injustice is atonement.
text.NRSV: for all these things are to be done because of the commandment.

text.KJV: 35:10 Giue vnto the most high, according as hee hath enriched thee, and as thou hast gotten, giue with a cheerefull eye.
text.NABRE: 35:10 With a generous spirit pay homage to the Lord, and do not spare your freewill gifts.
text.NRSV: Give to the Most High as he has given to you, and as generously as you can afford.

text.KJV: 35:11 For the Lord recompenseth, and will giue thee seuen times as much.
text.NABRE: 35:11 With each contribution show a cheerful countenance, and pay your tithes in a spirit of joy.
text.NRSV: For the Lord is the one who repays, and he will repay you sevenfold.

text.KJV: 35:15 Doeth not the teares run downe the widowes cheeks? and is not her crie against him that causeth them to fall?
text.NABRE: 35:15 Do not trust in sacrifice of the fruits of extortion, For he is a God of justice, who shows no partiality.
text.NRSV: Do not the tears of the widow run down her cheek as she cries out against the one who causes them to fall?

text.KJV: 35:17 The prayer of the humble pierceth the clouds: and till it come nigh he will not be comforted: and will not depart till the most High shall beholde to iudge righteously, and execute iudgement.
text.NABRE: 35:17 He does not forsake the cry of the orphan, nor the widow when she pours out her complaint.
text.NRSV: The prayer of the humble pierces the clouds, and it will not rest until it reaches its goal.

text.KJV: 36:1 Haue mercie vpon vs, O Lord God of all, and behold vs:
text.NABRE: 36:1 Chapter 36 A Prayer for God’s People - Come to our aid, O God of the universe,
text.NRSV: Have mercy upon us, O Lord, the God of all, and look upon us,

text.KJV: 36:2 And send thy feare vpon all the nations that seeke not after thee.
text.NABRE: 36:2 and put all the nations in dread of you!
text.NRSV: and put the fear of you upon all the nations.

text.KJV: 36:16 Reward them that wait for thee, and let thy prophets be found faithfull.
text.NRSV: Reward those who wait for you, and let your prophets be found trustworthy.

text.KJV: 36:21 A woman will receiue euery man, yet is one daughter better then another
text.NRSV: A woman will accept any man, but one girl is better than another.

text.KJV: 36:22 The beautie of a woman cheareth the countenance, and a man loueth nothing better.
text.NRSV: A woman’s beauty gladdens the countenance, and a man desires nothing more.

text.KJV: 36:24 He that getteth a wife, beginneth a possession, a helpe like vnto himselfe, and a pillar of rest.
text.NRSV: The man who acquires a wife gets his best possession, a helper fit for him and a pillar of support.

text.KJV: 36:25 Where no hedge is, there the possession is spoiled: and he that hath no wife will wander vp and downe mourning.
text.NRSV: Where there is no fence, the property will be plundered; and where there is no wife, a man will become a fugitive and a wanderer.

text.KJV: 37:1 Euery friend saieth, I am his friend also: but there is a friend which is onely a friend in name.
text.NABRE: 37:1 Chapter 37 - Every friend declares friendship, but there are friends who are friends in name only.
text.NRSV: Every friend says, “I too am a friend”; but some friends are friends only in name.

text.KJV: 37:2 Is it not a griefe vnto death, when a companion and friend is turned to an enemie?
text.NABRE: 37:2 Is it not a sorrow unto death when your other self becomes your enemy?
text.NRSV: Is it not a sorrow like that for death itself when a dear friend turns into an enemy?

text.KJV: 37:12 But be continually with a godly man, whom thou knowest to keepe the commandements of the Lord, whose minde is according to thy minde, and will sorrow with thee, if thou shalt miscarry.
text.NABRE: 37:12 Instead, associate with a religious person, who you know keeps the commandments; Who is like-minded with yourself and will grieve for you if you fall.
text.NRSV: But stay constantly with a God-fearing person whom you know to keep the commandments,

text.KJV: 37:13 And let the counsell of thine owne heart stand: for there is no man more faithfull vnto thee then it.
text.NABRE: 37:13 Then, too, heed your own heart’s counsel; for there is nothing you can depend on more.
text.NRSV: And heed the counsel of your own heart, for no one is more faithful to you than it is.

text.KJV: 37:14 For a mans minde is sometime wont to tell him more then seuen watchmen, that sit aboue in an high towre.
text.NABRE: 37:14 The heart can reveal your situation better than seven sentinels on a tower.
text.NRSV: For a person’s soul sometimes keeps him better informed than seven sentinels sitting high on a watchtower.

text.KJV: 37:27 My sonne prooue thy soule in thy life, and see what is euill for it, and giue not that vnto it.
text.NRSV: My child, test your soul while you live; see what is bad for it and do not give it that.

text.KJV: 37:28 For all things are not profitable for all men, neither hath euery soule pleasure in euery thing.
text.NRSV: For not everything is good for everyone, and no one enjoys everything.

text.KJV: 37:30 For excesse of meates, bringeth sicknesse, and surfetting will turne into choler.
text.NRSV: For overeating brings sickness, and gluttony leads to nausea.

text.KJV: 38:1 Honour a Phisitian with the honour due vnto him, for the vses which you may haue of him: for the Lord hath created him.
text.NABRE: 38:1 Chapter 38 Sickness and Death - Make friends with the doctor, for he is essential to you; God has also established him in his profession.
text.NRSV: Honor physicians for their services, for the Lord created them;

text.KJV: 38:2 For of the most High commeth healing, and he shall receiue honour of the King.
text.NABRE: 38:2 From God the doctor has wisdom, and from the king he receives sustenance.
text.NRSV: for their gift of healing comes from the Most High, and they are rewarded by the king.

text.KJV: 38:4 The Lord hath created medicines out of the earth; and he that is wise will not abhorre them.
text.NABRE: 38:4 God makes the earth yield healing herbs which the prudent should not neglect;
text.NRSV: The Lord created medicines out of the earth, and the sensible will not despise them.

text.KJV: 38:9 My sonne, in thy sickenesse be not negligent: but pray vnto the Lord, and he will make thee whole.
text.NABRE: 38:9 My son, when you are ill, do not delay, but pray to God, for it is he who heals.
text.NRSV: My child, when you are ill, do not delay, but pray to the Lord, and he will heal you.

text.KJV: 38:10 Leaue off from sinne, and order thy hands aright, and cleanse thy heart from all wickednesse.
text.NABRE: 38:10 Flee wickedness and purify your hands; cleanse your heart of every sin.
text.NRSV: Give up your faults and direct your hands rightly, and cleanse your heart from all sin.

text.KJV: 38:12 Then giue place to the phisitian, for the Lord hath created him: let him not go from thee, for thou hast need of him.
text.NABRE: 38:12 Then give the doctor his place lest he leave; you need him too,
text.NRSV: Then give the physician his place, for the Lord created him; and do not let him leave you, for you need him.

text.KJV: 38:16 My sonne, let teares fall downe ouer the dead, and begin to lament, as if thou hadst suffered great harme thy selfe: and then couer his body according to the custome, & neglect not his buriall.
text.NABRE: 38:16 My son, shed tears for one who is dead with wailing and bitter lament; As is only proper, prepare the body, and do not absent yourself from the burial.
text.NRSV: My child, let your tears fall for the dead, and as one who is suffering terribly begin the lament.

text.KJV: 38:21 Forget it not, for there is no turning againe: thou shalt not doe him good, but hurt thy selfe.
text.NABRE: 38:21 Do not recall him, for there is no hope of his return; you do him no good, and you harm yourself.
text.NRSV: Do not give your heart to sorrow; drive it away, and remember your own end.

text.KJV: 38:24 The wisedome of a learned man cōmeth by opportunitie of leasure: & he that hath litle busines shal become wise.
text.NABRE: 38:24 Vocations of the Skilled Worker and the Scribe - The scribe’s wisdom increases wisdom; whoever is free from toil can become wise.
text.NRSV: The wisdom of the scribe comes through opportunity of leisure; the one who has little business can become wise.

text.KJV: 39:1 But hee that giueth his minde to the Law of the most high, and is occupied in the meditation thereof, wil seeke out the wisdome of all the ancient, and be occupied in prophecies.
text.NABRE: 39:1 Chapter 39 - How different the person who devotes himself to the study of the law of the Most High! He explores the wisdom of all the ancients and is occupied with the prophecies;
text.NRSV: How different is the one who devotes himself to the study of the law of the Most High! He seeks out the wisdom of all the ancients, and is concerned with prophecies;

text.KJV: 39:2 Hee will keepe the sayings of the renowmed men: and where subtile parables are, he will be there also.
text.NABRE: 39:2 He preserves the discourses of the famous, and goes to the heart of involved sayings;
text.NRSV: he preserves the sayings of the famous and penetrates the subtleties of parables;

text.KJV: 39:6 When the great Lord will, he shall bee filled with the spirit of vnderstanding: he shal powre out wise sentences, and giue thankes vnto the Lord in his prayer.
text.NABRE: 39:6 If it pleases the Lord Almighty, he will be filled with the spirit of understanding; He will pour forth his words of wisdom and in prayer give praise to the Lord.
text.NRSV: If the great Lord is willing, he will be filled with the spirit of understanding; he will pour forth his words of wisdom and in prayer give thanks to the Lord.

text.KJV: 39:16 Al the works of the Lord are exceeding good, & whatsoeuer hee commandeth, shalbe accomplished in due season.
text.NABRE: 39:16 The works of God are all of them good; he supplies for every need in its own time.
text.NRSV: “All the works of the Lord are very good, and whatever he commands will be done at the appointed time.”

text.KJV: 39:26 The principall things for the whole vse of mans life, are water, fire, yron, and salt, floure of wheate, honie, milke, and the blood of the grape, and oyle, and clothing.
text.NABRE: 39:26 Chief of all needs for human life are water and fire, iron and salt, The heart of the wheat, milk and honey, the blood of the grape, and oil, and clothing.
text.NRSV: Basic to all the needs of human life are water and fire and iron and salt and wheat flour and milk and honey, the blood of the grape, and oil and clothing.

text.KJV: 39:27 All these things are for good to the godly: so to the sinners they are turned into euill.
text.NABRE: 39:27 For the good all these are good, but for the wicked they turn out evil.
text.NRSV: All these are good for the godly, but for sinners they turn into evils.

text.KJV: 40:1 Great trauaile is created for euery man, and an heauy yoke is vpon the sons of Adam, from the day that they goe out of their mothers wombe, till the day that they returne to the mother of all things.
text.NABRE: 40:1 Chapter 40 Joys and Miseries of Life - A great anxiety has God allotted, and a heavy yoke, to the children of Adam, From the day they leave their mother’s womb until the day they return to the mother of all the living.
text.NRSV: Hard work was created for everyone, and a heavy yoke is laid on the children of Adam, from the day they come forth from their mother’s womb until the day they return to the mother of all the living.

text.KJV: 40:11 All things that are of the earth shal turne to the earth againe: and that which is of the waters doeth returne into the Sea.
text.NABRE: 40:11 All that is of earth returns to earth, and what is from above returns above.
text.NRSV: All that is of earth returns to earth, and what is from the waters returns to the sea.

text.KJV: 40:17 Bountifulnes is as a most fruitfull garden, and mercifulnesse endureth for euer.
text.NABRE: 40:17 But goodness, like eternity, will never be cut off, and righteousness endures forever.
text.NRSV: Kindness is like a garden of blessings, and almsgiving endures forever.

text.KJV: 40:18 To labour & to be content with that a man hath, is a sweet life: but hee that findeth a treasure, is aboue them both.
text.NABRE: 40:18 Wealth or wages can make life sweet, but better than either, finding a treasure.
text.NRSV: To be self-sufficient and to be a worker—both are sweet, but better than both is the one who finds a treasure.

text.KJV: 40:20 Wine & musicke reioyce the heart: but the loue of wisedome is aboue them both.
text.NABRE: 40:20 Wine and strong drink delight the soul, but better than either, love of friends.
text.NRSV: Wine and music gladden the heart, but the love of wisdom is better than both.

text.KJV: 40:21 The pipe and the psalterie make sweet melodie: but a pleasant tongue is aboue them both.
text.NABRE: 40:21 Flute and harp offer sweet melody, but better than either, a pure tongue.
text.NRSV: Flute and harp make pleasant melody, but a gentle tongue is better than both.

text.KJV: 40:23 A friend and companion neuer meet amisse: but aboue both is a wife with her husband.
text.NABRE: 40:23 A friend and a neighbor are timely guides, but better than either, a sensible wife.
text.NRSV: A friend and a companion encounter each other at the opportune moment, but better than both is a wife with her husband.

text.KJV: 41:1 O death, how bitter is the remembrance of thee to a man that liueth at rest in his possessions, vnto the man that hath nothing to vexe him, and that hath prosperity in all things: yea vnto him that is yet able to receiue meate?
text.NABRE: 41:1 Chapter 41 - O death! How bitter is the thought of you for the one at peace in his home, For the one who is serene and always successful, who can still enjoy life’s pleasures.
text.NRSV: O death, how bitter is the thought of you to the one who lives at peace among his possessions,

text.KJV: 41:2 O death, acceptable is thy sentence vnto the needy, and vnto him whose strength faileth, that is now in the last age, and is vexed with all things, and to him that despaireth and hath lost patience.
text.NABRE: 41:2 O death! How welcome is your sentence to the weak, failing in strength, Stumbling and tripping on everything, with sight gone and hope lost.
text.NRSV: O death, how welcome is your sentence to one who is in need and is failing in strength,

text.KJV: 41:3 Feare not the sentence of death, remember them that haue beene before thee, and that come after, for this is the sentence of the Lord ouer all flesh.
text.NABRE: 41:3 Do not fear death’s decree for you; remember, it embraces those before you and those to come.
text.NRSV: Do not fear the sentence of death; remember those who went before you and those who will come after.

text.KJV: 41:12 Haue regard to thy name: for that shall continue with thee aboue a thousand great treasures of gold.
text.NABRE: 41:12 Have respect for your name, for it will stand by you more than thousands of precious treasures.
text.NRSV: Have regard for your name, since it will remain for you longer than a thousand great treasures of gold.

text.KJV: 41:13 A good life hath but few daies: but a good name endureth for euer.
text.NABRE: 41:13 The good things of life last a number of days, but a good name, for days without number.
text.NRSV: The days of a good life are numbered, but a good name lasts forever.

text.KJV: 42:1 Of these things be not thou ashamed, and accept no person to sinne thereby.
text.NABRE: 42:1 Chapter 42 - Of repeating what you hear, of betraying any secret. Be ashamed of the right things, and you will find favor in the sight of all. But of these things do not be ashamed, lest you sin to save face:
text.NRSV: Of the following things do not be ashamed, and do not let partiality lead you to sin:

text.KJV: 42:2 Of the Law of the most High, and his Couenant, and of iudgement to iustifie the vngodly:
text.NABRE: 42:2 Of the Law of the Most High and his precepts, or of justice that acquits the ungodly;
text.NRSV: of the law of the Most High and his covenant, and of justice to acquit the ungodly;

text.KJV: 42:15 I will now remember the works of the Lord, and declare the things that I haue seene: in the words of the Lord are his workes.
text.NABRE: 42:15 The Works of God in Nature - Now will I recall God’s works; what I have seen, I will describe. By the LORD’s word his works were brought into being; he accepts the one who does his will.
text.NRSV: I will now call to mind the works of the Lord, and will declare what I have seen. By the words of the Lord his works are done.

text.KJV: 42:18 He seeketh out the deepe and the heart, and considereth their crafty deuices: for the Lord knoweth all that may be knowen, and he beholdeth the signes of the world.
text.NABRE: 42:18 He searches out the abyss and penetrates the heart; their secrets he understands. For the Most High possesses all knowledge, and sees from of old the things that are to come.
text.NRSV: He searches out the abyss and the human heart; he understands their innermost secrets.

text.KJV: 42:24 All things are double one against another: and hee hath made nothing vnperfit.
text.NABRE: 42:24 All of them differ, one from another, yet none of them has he made in vain;
text.NRSV: All things are in pairs, one opposite the other, and he has made nothing incomplete.

text.KJV: 43:1 The pride of the height, the cleare firmament, the beautie of heauen, with his glorious shew;
text.NABRE: 43:1 Chapter 43 - The beauty of the celestial height and the pure firmament, heaven itself manifests its glory.
text.NRSV: The pride of the higher realms is the clear firmament, the appearance of heaven in a spectacle of glory.

text.KJV: 43:2 The Sunne when it appeareth, declaring at his rising, a marueilous instrument, the worke of the most High.
text.NABRE: 43:2 The sun at its rising shines at its fullest, a wonderful instrument, the work of the Most High!
text.NRSV: The sun, when it appears, proclaims as it rises what a marvelous instrument it is, the work of the Most High.

text.KJV: 43:6 He made the Moone also to serue in her season, for a declaration of times, and a signe of the world.
text.NABRE: 43:6 It is the moon that marks the changing seasons, governing the times, their lasting sign.
text.NRSV: The moon also, at its correct time, is the guide of the seasons and an everlasting sign.

text.KJV: 43:11 Looke vpon the rainebow, and praise him that made it, very beautifull it is in the brightnesse thereof.
text.NABRE: 43:11 Behold the rainbow! Then bless its Maker, for majestic indeed is its splendor;
text.NRSV: Look at the rainbow, and praise him who made it; it is exceedingly beautiful in its brightness.

text.KJV: 43:13 By his commandement hee maketh the snow to fall apace, and sendeth swiftly the lightnings of his iudgment.
text.NABRE: 43:13 His rebuke marks out the path for the hail, and makes the flashes of his judgment shine forth.
text.NRSV: By his command he sends the driving snow and speeds the lightnings of his judgment.

text.KJV: 43:27 We may speake much, & yet come short: wherefore in summe, he is all.
text.NABRE: 43:27 More than this we need not add; let the last word be, he is the all!
text.NRSV: We could say more but could never say enough; let the final word be, “He is the all.”

text.KJV: 43:30 When you glorifie the Lord exalt him as much as you can: for euen yet wil he farre exceed, and when you exalt him, put foorth all your strength, and be not weary: for you can neuer goe farre enough.
text.NABRE: 43:30 Lift up your voices to glorify the LORD as much as you can, for there is still more. Extol him with renewed strength, do not grow weary, for you cannot fathom him.
text.NRSV: Exalt him as much as you can, for he will surpass even that; and when you exalt him, summon all your strength, and do not grow weary, for you can never exalt him enough.

text.KJV: 44:1 Let vs now praise famous men, and our Fathers that begat vs.
text.NABRE: 44:1 Chapter 44 Praise of Israel’s Great Ancestors - I will now praise the godly, our ancestors, in their own time,
text.NRSV: Let us now sing the praises of famous men, our ancestors in their generations.

text.KJV: 44:2 The Lorde hath wrought great glory by them, through his great power from the beginning.
text.NABRE: 44:2 The abounding glory of the Most High’s portion, his own part, since the days of old.
text.NRSV: The Lord apportioned to them great glory, his majesty from the beginning.

text.KJV: 44:9 And some there be, which haue no memorial, who are perished as though they had neuer bene, and are become as though they had neuer bene borne, and their children after them.
text.NABRE: 44:9 Of others no memory remains, for when they perished, they perished, As if they had never lived, they and their children after them.
text.NRSV: But of others there is no memory; they have perished as though they had never existed;

text.KJV: 44:10 But these were mercifull men, whose righteousnesse hath not beene forgotten.
text.NABRE: 44:10 Yet these also were godly; their virtues have not been forgotten.
text.NRSV: But these also were godly men, whose righteous deeds have not been forgotten;

text.KJV: 44:13 Their seed shall remaine for euer, and their glory shall not be blotted out.
text.NABRE: 44:13 And for all time their progeny will endure, their glory will never be blotted out;
text.NRSV: Their offspring will continue forever, and their glory will never be blotted out.

text.KJV: 44:14 Their bodies are buried in peace, but their name liueth for euermore.
text.NABRE: 44:14 Their bodies are buried in peace, but their name lives on and on.
text.NRSV: Their bodies are buried in peace, but their name lives on to generation after generation.

text.KJV: 44:16 Enoch pleased the Lord, and was translated, being an example of repentance, to all generations.
text.NABRE: 44:16 The Early Ancestors - [ENOCH walked with the LORD and was taken, that succeeding generations might learn by his example.]
text.NRSV: Enoch pleased the Lord and was taken up, an example of repentance to all generations.

text.KJV: 44:17 Noah was found perfect and righteous, in the time of wrath, he was taken in exchange (for the world) therefore was he left as a remnant vnto the earth, when the flood came.
text.NABRE: 44:17 NOAH, found just and perfect, renewed the race in the time of devastation. Because of his worth there were survivors, and with a sign to him the deluge ended.
text.NRSV: Noah was found perfect and righteous; in the time of wrath he kept the race alive.

text.KJV: 44:19 Abraham was a great father of many people: in glory was there none like vnto him:
text.NABRE: 44:19 ABRAHAM, father of many peoples, kept his glory without stain:
text.NRSV: Abraham was the great father of a multitude of nations, and no one has been found like him in glory.

text.KJV: 45:1 And he brought out of him a mercifull man, which found fauour in the sight of all flesh, euen Moses beloued of God and men, whose memoriall is blessed:
text.NABRE: 45:1 Chapter 45 Praise of Moses, Aaron, and Phinehas - From him came the man who would win the favor of all the living: Dear to God and human beings, MOSES, whose memory is a blessing.
text.NRSV: From Abraham’s descendants the Lord brought forth a godly man, who found favor in the sight of all and was beloved by God and people, Moses, whose memory is blessed.

text.KJV: 45:2 He made him like to the glorious Saints, and magnified him, so that his enemies stood in feare of him.
text.NABRE: 45:2 God made him like the angels in honor, and strengthened him with fearful powers.
text.NRSV: He made him equal in glory to the holy ones, and made him great, to the terror of his enemies.

text.KJV: 45:5 He made him to heare his voyce, and brought him into the darke cloud, and gaue him commandements before his face, euen the law of life and knowledge, that hee might teach Iacob his Couenants, and Israel his iudgments.
text.NABRE: 45:5 He let him hear his voice, and led him into the cloud, Where he handed over the commandments, the law of life and understanding, That he might teach his precepts to Jacob, his judgments and decrees to Israel.
text.NRSV: He allowed him to hear his voice, and led him into the thick darkness, and gave him the commandments face to face.

text.KJV: 45:6 He exalted Aaron an holy man like vnto him, euen his brother, of the tribe of Leui.
text.NABRE: 45:6 He also raised up, like Moses in holiness, his brother AARON, of the tribe of Levi.
text.NRSV: He exalted Aaron, a holy man like Moses who was his brother, of the tribe of Levi.

text.KJV: 45:15 Moises consecrated him, and annointed him with holy oile, this was appointed vnto him by an euerlasting couenant, and to his seed so long as the heauens should remaine, that they should minister vnto him, and execute the office of the priesthood, and blesse the people in his name.
text.NABRE: 45:15 For Moses ordained him and anointed him with the holy oil, In a lasting covenant with him and his family, as permanent as the heavens, That he should serve God in the priesthood and bless the people in his name.
text.NRSV: Moses ordained him and anointed him with holy oil; this was an everlasting covenant for him and for his descendants as long as the heavens endure.

text.KJV: 46:1 Iesus the sonne of Naue was valiant in the wars, and was the successor of Moses in prophesies, who according to his name was made great for the sauing of the elect of God, and taking vengeance of the enemies that rose vp against them, that he might set Israel in their inheritance.
text.NABRE: 46:1 Chapter 46 Joshua, Caleb, the Judges, and Samuel - Valiant warrior was JOSHUA, son of Nun, aide to Moses in the prophetic office, Formed to be, as his name implies, the great savior of God’s chosen ones, To punish the enemy and to give to Israel their heritage.
text.NRSV: Joshua son of Nun was mighty in war, and was the successor of Moses in prophesying. He became, in accordance with his name, a great savior of God’s elect.

text.KJV: 46:7 In the time of Moses also, he did a worke of mercie, hee and Caleb the sonne of Iephunne, in that they withstood the Congregation, and withheld the people from sinne, and appeased the wicked murmuring.
text.NABRE: 46:7 and showed himself loyal in Moses’ lifetime. He and CALEB, son of Jephunneh, when they opposed the rebel assembly, Averted God’s anger from the people and suppressed the wicked complaint.
text.NRSV: In the days of Moses also he did a loyal deed, he and Caleb son of Jephunneh: they withstood the congregation, restrained the people from sin, and stilled the wicked grumbling.

text.KJV: 46:11 And concerning the Iudges, euery one by name, whose heart went not a whoring, nor departed from the Lord, let their memory be blessed.
text.NABRE: 46:11 The JUDGES, each one of them, whose hearts were not deceived, Who did not abandon God— may their memory be ever blessed!
text.NRSV: The judges also, with their respective names, whose hearts did not fall into idolatry and who did not turn away from the Lord—may their memory be blessed!

text.KJV: 46:13 Samuel the Prophet of the Lord, beloued of his Lord, established a kingdom, & anointed princes ouer his people.
text.NABRE: 46:13 Beloved of his people, dear to his Maker, pledged in a vow from his mother’s womb, As one consecrated to the LORD in the prophetic office, was SAMUEL, the judge who offered sacrifice. At God’s word he established the kingdom and anointed princes to rule the people.
text.NRSV: Samuel was beloved by his Lord; a prophet of the Lord, he established the kingdom and anointed rulers over his people.

text.KJV: 46:20 And after his death he prophesied, and shewed the King his end, and lift vp his voyce from the earth in prophesie, to blot out the wickednesse of the people.
text.NABRE: 46:20 Even after death his guidance was sought; he made known to the king his fate. From the grave he spoke in prophecy to put an end to wickedness.
text.NRSV: Even after he had fallen asleep, he prophesied and foretold to the king his death, and lifted up his voice from the ground in prophecy, to blot out the wickedness of the people.

text.KJV: 47:1 And after him rose vp Nathan to prophesie in the time of Dauid.
text.NABRE: 47:1 Chapter 47 Nathan, David, and Solomon - After him came NATHAN who served in David’s presence.
text.NRSV: After him Nathan rose up to prophesy in the days of David.

text.KJV: 47:2 As is the fat taken away from the peace offering, so was Dauid chosen out of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: 47:2 Like the choice fat of sacred offerings, so was DAVID in Israel.
text.NRSV: As the fat is set apart from the offering of well-being, so David was set apart from the Israelites.

text.KJV: 47:8 In all his workes hee praised the holy one most High, with words of glory, with his whole heart he sung songs, and loued him that made him.
text.NABRE: 47:8 With his every deed he offered thanks to God Most High, in words of praise. With his whole heart he loved his Maker
text.NRSV: In all that he did he gave thanks to the Holy One, the Most High, with ascriptions of glory; he sang praise with all his heart, and he loved his Maker.

text.KJV: 47:11 The Lord tooke away his sinnes, and exalted his horne for euer: he gaue him a couenant of kings, and a throne of glory in Israel.
text.NABRE: 47:11 The LORD forgave him his sins and exalted his strength forever; He conferred on him the rights of royalty and established his throne in Israel.
text.NRSV: The Lord took away his sins, and exalted his power forever; he gave him a covenant of kingship and a glorious throne in Israel.

text.KJV: 47:12 After him rose vp a wise sonne, and for his sake he dwelt at large.
text.NABRE: 47:12 Because of his merits he had as successor a wise son, who lived in security:
text.NRSV: After him a wise son rose up who because of him lived in security.

text.KJV: 47:13 Salomon reigned in a peaceable time, and was honoured; for God made all quiet round about him, that hee might build an house in his Name, and prepare his Sanctuary for euer.
text.NABRE: 47:13 SOLOMON reigned during an era of peace, for God brought rest to all his borders. He built a house to the name of God, and established a lasting sanctuary.
text.NRSV: Solomon reigned in an age of peace, because God gave him rest on every side, so that he might build a house for his name and prepare a sanctuary to stand forever.

text.KJV: 47:14 How wise wast thou in thy youth, & as a flood filled with vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: 47:14 How wise you were when you were young, overflowing with instruction, like the Nile in flood!
text.NRSV: How wise you were when you were young! You overflowed like the Nile with understanding.

text.KJV: 48:1 Then stood vp Elias the Prophet as fire, and his word burnt like a lampe.
text.NABRE: 48:1 Chapter 48 - Until like fire a prophet appeared, his words a flaming furnace.
text.NRSV: Then Elijah arose, a prophet like fire, and his word burned like a torch.

text.KJV: 48:4 O Elias, how wast thou honoured in thy wondrous deedes! and who may glory like vnto thee!
text.NABRE: 48:4 How awesome are you, ELIJAH! Whose glory is equal to yours?
text.NRSV: How glorious you were, Elijah, in your wondrous deeds! Whose glory is equal to yours?

text.KJV: 48:11 Blessed are they that saw thee, and slept in loue, for we shal surely liue.
text.NABRE: 48:11 Blessed is the one who shall have seen you before he dies!
text.NRSV: Happy are those who saw you and were adorned with your love! For we also shall surely live.

text.KJV: 48:12 Elias it was, who was couered with a whirlewinde: and Elizeus was filled with his spirit: whilest he liued he was not mooued of any prince, neither could any bring him into subiection.
text.NABRE: 48:12 When Elijah was enveloped in the whirlwind, ELISHA was filled with his spirit; He worked twice as many marvels, and every utterance of his mouth was wonderful. During his lifetime he feared no one, nor was anyone able to intimidate his will.
text.NRSV: When Elijah was enveloped in the whirlwind, Elisha was filled with his spirit; he performed twice as many signs, and marvels with every utterance of his mouth.

text.KJV: 48:13 No word could ouercome him, & after his death his body prophecied.
text.NABRE: 48:13 Nothing was beyond his power; and from where he lay buried, his body prophesied.
text.NRSV: Nothing was too hard for him, and when he was dead, his body prophesied.

text.KJV: 48:20 But they called vpon the Lord which is mercifull, and stretched out their hands towards him, and immediatly the holy One heard them out of heauen, and deliuered them by the ministery of Esay.
text.NABRE: 48:20 But they called upon the Most High God and lifted up their hands to him; He heard the prayer they uttered, and saved them through ISAIAH.
text.NRSV: But they called upon the Lord who is merciful, spreading out their hands toward him. The Holy One quickly heard them from heaven, and delivered them by the hand of Isaiah.

text.KJV: 48:22 For Ezekias had done the thing that pleased the Lord, and was strong in the wayes of Dauid his father, as Esay the Prophet, who was great and faithfull in his vision, had commaunded him.
text.NABRE: 48:22 For Hezekiah did what was right and held fast to the paths of David, As ordered by the illustrious prophet Isaiah, who saw truth in visions.
text.NRSV: For Hezekiah did what was pleasing to the Lord, and he kept firmly to the ways of his ancestor David, as he was commanded by the prophet Isaiah, who was great and trustworthy in his visions.

text.KJV: 49:1 The remembrance of Iosias is like the composition of the perfume þ is made by the arte of the Apothecarie: it is sweete as hony in all mouthes, and as musicke at a banquet of wine.
text.NABRE: 49:1 Chapter 49 Josiah and the Prophets - The name JOSIAH is like blended incense, made lasting by a skilled perfumer. Precious is his memory, like honey to the taste, like music at a banquet.
text.NRSV: The memory of Josiah is like a blending of incense prepared by the art of the perfumer; it is sweet as honey to every mouth, and like music at a banquet of wine.

text.KJV: 49:4 All, except Dauid and Ezechias, and Iosias, were defectiue: for they forsooke the Law of the most High, (euen) the kings of Iudah failed:
text.NABRE: 49:4 Except for David, Hezekiah, and Josiah, they all were wicked; They abandoned the Law of the Most High, these kings of Judah, right to the very end.
text.NRSV: Except for David and Hezekiah and Josiah, all of them were great sinners, for they abandoned the law of the Most High; the kings of Judah came to an end.

text.KJV: 49:8 It was Ezechiel who sawe the glorious vision, which was shewed him vpon the chariot of the Cherubims
text.NABRE: 49:8 EZEKIEL beheld a vision and described the different creatures of the chariot;
text.NRSV: It was Ezekiel who saw the vision of glory, which God showed him above the chariot of the cherubim.

text.KJV: 49:10 And of the twelue prophets let the memorial be blessed, and let their bones flourish againe out of their place: for they comforted Iacob, and deliuered them by assured hope.
text.NABRE: 49:10 Then, too, the TWELVE PROPHETS— may their bones flourish with new life where they lie!— They gave new strength to Jacob and saved him with steadfast hope.
text.NRSV: May the bones of the twelve prophets send forth new life from where they lie, for they comforted the people of Jacob and delivered them with confident hope.

text.KJV: 49:11 How shall we magnifie Zorobabel? euen he was as a signet on the right hand.
text.NABRE: 49:11 The Heroes After the Exile - How to extol ZERUBBABEL? He was like a signet ring on the right hand,
text.NRSV: How shall we magnify Zerubbabel? He was like a signet ring on the right hand;

text.KJV: 49:12 So was Iesus the sonne of Iosedec: who in their time builded the house, and set vp an holy Temple to the Lord, which was prepared for euerlasting glory.
text.NABRE: 49:12 And JESHUA, Jozadak’s son? In their time they rebuilt the altar And erected the holy temple, destined for everlasting glory.
text.NRSV: so too was Joshua son of Jozadak; in their days they built the house and raised a temple holy to the Lord, prepared for everlasting glory.

text.KJV: 49:13 And among the elect was Neemias whose renowme is great, who raised vp for vs, the walles that were fallen, and set vp the gates & the barres, and raised vp our ruines againe.
text.NABRE: 49:13 Exalted be the memory of NEHEMIAH! He rebuilt our ruined walls, Restored our shattered defenses, and set up gates and bars.
text.NRSV: The memory of Nehemiah also is lasting; he raised our fallen walls, and set up gates and bars, and rebuilt our ruined houses.

text.KJV: 50:1 Simon the high priest the sonne of Onias, who in his life repaired the house againe, and in his dayes fortified the Temple:
text.NABRE: 50:1 Chapter 50 Simeon, Son of Jochanan - Greatest of his family, the glory of his people, was SIMEON the priest, son of Jochanan, In whose time the house of God was renovated, in whose days the temple was reinforced.
text.NRSV: The leader of his brothers and the pride of his people was the high priest, Simon son of Onias, who in his life repaired the house, and in his time fortified the temple.

text.KJV: 50:5 How was he honoured in the midst of the people, in his comming out of the Sanctuarie?
text.NABRE: 50:5 How splendid he was as he looked out from the tent, as he came from behind the veil!
text.NRSV: How glorious he was, surrounded by the people, as he came out of the house of the curtain.

text.KJV: 50:22 Now therefore blesse yee the God of all, which onely doth wonderous things euery where, which exalteth our daies from the wombe, and dealeth with vs according to his mercy.
text.NABRE: 50:22 And now, bless the God of all, who has done wonders on earth; Who fosters growth from the womb, fashioning it according to his will!
text.NRSV: And now bless the God of all, who everywhere works great wonders, who fosters our growth from birth, and deals with us according to his mercy.

text.KJV: 50:23 He grant vs ioyfulnesse of heart, and that peace may be in our daies in Israel for euer.
text.NABRE: 50:23 May he grant you a wise heart and abide with you in peace;
text.NRSV: May he give us gladness of heart, and may there be peace in our days in Israel, as in the days of old.

text.KJV: 50:27 Iesus the sonne of Sirach of Hierusalem hath written in this booke, the instruction of vnderstanding and knowledge, who out of his heart powred forth wisedome.
text.NABRE: 50:27 Wise instruction, appropriate proverbs, I have written in this book— I, Yeshua Ben Eleazar Ben Sira— as they poured forth from my heart’s understanding.
text.NRSV: Instruction in understanding and knowledge I have written in this book, I, Jesus son of Eleazar son of Sirach of Jerusalem, whose heart poured forth wisdom.

text.KJV: 51:1 I will thanke thee, O Lord and king, and praise thee O God my Sauiour, I doe giue praise vnto thy name:
text.NABRE: 51:1 Chapter 51 A Prayer of Thanksgiving - I give you thanks, LORD and King, I praise you, God my savior! I declare your name, refuge of my life,
text.NRSV: I will give thanks to you, O Lord and King, and praise you, O God my Savior. I give thanks to your name;

text.KJV: 51:2 For thou art my defeuder, and helper, and hast preserued my body from destruction, and from the snare of the slanderous tongue, and from the lippes that forge lies, and hast beene my helper against mine aduersaries.
text.NABRE: 51:2 because you have ransomed my life from death; You held back my body from the pit, and delivered my foot from the power of Sheol. You have preserved me from the scourge of the slanderous tongue, and from the lips of those who went over to falsehood. You were with me against those who rise up against me;
text.NRSV: for you have been my protector and helper and have delivered me from destruction.

text.KJV: 51:13 When I was yet yong, or euer I went abroad, I desired wisedome openly in my prayer.
text.NABRE: 51:13 Ben Sira’s Pursuit of Wisdom - When I was young and innocent, I sought wisdom.
text.NRSV: While I was still young, before I went on my travels, I sought wisdom openly in my prayer.

text.KJV: 51:17 I profited therein, will I ascribe the glory vnto him that giueth me wisedome:
text.NABRE: 51:17 Since in this way I have profited, I will give my Teacher grateful praise.
text.NRSV: I made progress in her; to him who gives me wisdom I will give glory.

text.KJV: 51:23 Draw neere vnto me you vnlearned, and dwell in the house of learning.
text.NABRE: 51:23 Come aside to me, you untutored, and take up lodging in the house of instruction;
text.NRSV: Draw near to me, you who are uneducated, and lodge in the house of instruction.

text.KJV: 51:26 Put your necke vnder the yoke, and let your soule receiue instruction, she is hard at hand to finde.
text.NABRE: 51:26 Take her yoke upon your neck; that your mind may receive her teaching. For she is close to those who seek her, and the one who is in earnest finds her.
text.NRSV: Put your neck under her yoke, and let your souls receive instruction; it is to be found close by.

text.KJV: 51:27 Behold with your eies, how that I haue had but little labour, and haue gotten vnto me much rest.
text.NABRE: 51:27 See for yourselves! I have labored only a little, but have found much.
text.NRSV: See with your own eyes that I have labored but little and found for myself much serenity.

text.KJV: 51:30 Worke your worke betimes, & in his time he will giue you your reward.
text.NABRE: 51:30 Work at your tasks in due season, and in his own time God will give you your reward.
text.NRSV: Do your work in good time, and in his own time God will give you your reward.
